Items.Title: 这是令人兴奋的：所有事物 DevOps 的新目的地 | This is Exciting: A New Destination for All Things DevOps
Items.Description:
我很高兴能重点介绍一个新的 Forem： Ops 社区。 这是一个专门为 DevOps、SecOps、IT 经理、SRE 和其他云原生专业人士打造的新空间。

如果您从事 DevOps 工作，您就会知道这是一个快速发展的领域，紧跟潮流是至关重要的——但这可能很困难，而且势不可挡。 无论好信息会在大量 JavaScript 教程中丢失，还是仅限于聊天空间和 Twitter 线程，将信号与噪音分开对您的职业生涯至关重要，并且需要更容易一些。

我们昨天邀请了一些人加入，这个空间已经很繁荣了。

如果您是一名全面的 DevOps 专业人士，或者只是倾向于 IT 领域的那个方向，那么这对您来说将是一个非常有用的资源和社区。 请花点时间立即加入并在欢迎帖中发帖。

感谢Blink管理这个社区！

快乐的操作！ 最后一件事。 您会注意到“使用 Forem 注册”作为这些 Forem 的一个选项。 这是我们现在提供的一项服务，可以最无缝地导航生态系统并管理您的身份。 我们希望您能够在无需携带全部数据的情况下充分发挥自我，因此从长远来看，使这个分布式生态系统尽可能简单是非常重要的。 创建一个Forem 帐户并通过您的设置将其与您的 DEV 帐户连接。 🙂 🙂



原文:

I am very excited to highlight a new Forem: The Ops Community. It is a new space dedicated to DevOps, SecOps, IT Managers, SREs, and other cloud-native professionals.

If you work in DevOps, you know that it is a fast-moving field where keeping up with the pulse is critical — but that can be difficult and overwhelming. Whether good information gets lost in a sea of JavaScript tutorials or is confined to chat spaces and Twitter threads, separating signal from noise is critical for your career and needs to be just a bit easier.

We invited some folks yesterday to join, and the space is already thriving.

The Ops Community is a place for cloud engineers of all experience levels to share tips & tricks, tutorials, and career insights.

If you are a full-on DevOps professional, or simply trend in that direction of the IT spectrum, this is going to be a very useful resource and community for you. Please take a moment to join right now and post in the Welcome Thread.

Thank you to Blink for stewarding this community!

Happy Opsing! 🎉

One last thing. You’ll notice "Sign up with Forem" as an option on these Forems. This is a service we now offer to most seamlessly navigate the ecosystem and manage your identity. We want you to be able to bring your full self without having to bring your full data, so making this distributed ecosystem as straightforward as possible in the long run is really important. Create a Forem account and connect it with your DEV account via your settings. 🙂
Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: Pantangan Setelah 填料 | Pantangan Setelah Filler
Items.Description:
琼脂 mendapatkan hasil seperti yang diinginkan，ada beberapa hal yang menjadi pantangan setelah 填料。 阿帕萨哈？ 皮肤填充剂 banyak digemari orang karena salah satu alasan, yaitu aman。 琼脂 tidak merusak hasil perawatan 填料，sebaiknya kamu juga tidak melakukan ciuman terlebih dahulu。



原文:

Pantangan Setelah Filler Dagu – Saat ini, Derma Filler merupakan sebuah perawatan yang sangat banyak digemari oleh orang, terutama wanita. Hal ini disebabkan karena perawatan filler, dianggap memberikan hasil yang sangat baik bagi wajah. Khususnya bagi mereka yang ingin tetap tampil dengan awet muda.

Biasanya orang melakukan suntik derma filler bertujuan untuk menyamarkan kerutan dan garis halus di wajah. Selain itu, derma filler juga dapat diupayakan untuk meratakan tekstur pada kulit, menghaluskan kulit, dan menghilangkan bekas luka yang ada pada kulit. Agar mendapatkan hasil seperti yang diinginkan, ada beberapa hal yang menjadi pantangan setelah filler. Apa saja? Berikut adalah ulasannya.

**Sekilas Informasi Mengenai Suntik Derma Filler **Suntik filler adalah sebuah perawatan yang dilakukan untuk memperbaiki atau mengoreksi area tertentu yang dirasa memang membutuhkan. Pada tindakan prosedurnya, filler dilakukan dengan metode injeksi, dan biasanya dokter membutuhkan waktu kurang lebih 30 menit untuk menyelesaikan prosedur ini. Hasil injeksi dari filler biasanya bertahan sekitar enam bulan hingga satu tahun lamanya. Pada masa ini, kamu sebaiknya juga harus mengetahui tentang pantangan setelah filler dagu. Dermal filler banyak digemari orang karena salah satu alasan, yaitu aman. Sebab, perawatan ini hanya boleh dilakukan oleh dokter spesialis kulit, dokter bedah kecantikan, atau terapis kecantikan yang kompeten. Lebih spesifik, dokter yang menangani filler juga harus memiliki sertifikat ahli dalam prosedur kecantikan ini.

Suntik filler mungkin akan menimbulkan beberapa efek samping di area suntikan seperti kemerahan, bengkak, nyeri, gatal, dan infeksi. Selain itu, efek samping lain dari filler adalah reaksi alergi, emboli pembuluh darah, perpindahan filler, dan luka jaringan parut (keloid). Oleh karena itu, konsultasikan terlebih dahulu dengan dokter Anda sebelum melakukan filler. Hal ini dilakukan untuk menghindari atau mengurangi efek samping dan risiko yang mungkin terjadi.

**Pantangan setelah melakukan suntik filler
**Walaupun Derma Filler merupakan sebuah perawatan yang aman, namun pasien yang melakukan filler memiliki beberapa pantangan yang harus diperhatikan. Pantangan ini, akan kami jelaskan menurut pada area yang biasanya dilakukan injeksi. Berikut adalah penjelasan lebih lengkapnya:

**Pantangan Setelah Filler Dagu
**Setelah melakukan injeksi filler dagu, dokter biasanya akan memberi saran agar pasien tidak menyentuh, memencet, meminjat, atau menggosok area dagu ataupun sekitarnya. Sebisa mungkin, terapkan hal ini setidaknya satu hari setelah suntik filler dilakukan. Hal ini bertujuan untuk mencegah kerusakan dan terjadinya perpindahan filler yang telah disuntikkan.

Kamu juga tidak dianjurkan untuk melakukan perawatan pada area wajah, setidaknya dua hari setelah prosedur suntik filler dilakukan. Hindari juga memakai produk perawatan kecantikan apa pun atau menggunakan riasan pada area wajah setelah melakukan suntik filler.

Selain hal yang telah disebutkan di atas, kamu juga tidak dibolehkan untuk melakukan aktivitas berat. Contohnya seperti berolahraga, berhubungan seks, ataupun aktivitas fisik lain yang memerlukan tenaga ekstra. Hal ini disebabkan karena setiap aktivitas yang meningkatkan tekanan darah dan detak jantung, dapat membuat atau menimbulkan memar.

Pasien derma filler sebaiknya juga menghindari minum alkohol dan berbagai minuman yang mengandung kafein. Selain itu, hindari juga makanan yang tinggi natrium (garam), gula, dan karbohidrat, serta makanan pedas. Tujuan dari pantangan setelah filler dagu ini, berupaya agar tidak terjadi kerusakan pada area tindakan. Dengan lebih berhati-hati, perawatan derma filler yang kamu lakukan pun akan membuat hasil yang lebih maksimal.

**Pantangan setelah filler bibir
**Jika kamu melakukan suntik filler pada area bibir, ada beberapa pantangan yang juga harus kamu perhatikan. Salah satunya adalah aktivitas yang menimbulkan panas pada area bibir dan dapat merusak hasil filler. Oleh sebab itu, dianjurkan bagi pasien untuk tidak merokok, minum minuman yang terlalu panas, dan sauna setelah melakukan injeksi filler.

Selain itu, kamu juga disarankan untuk tidak menggunakan lipstik atau berbagai produk kosmetik bibir lainnya. Setidaknya, lakukan anjuran ini setelah satu hari melakukan suntik filler. Agar tidak merusak hasil perawatan filler, sebaiknya kamu juga tidak melakukan ciuman terlebih dahulu.
Items.Author: beautynewsindo

Items.Title: 启动 SideProject 时要避免的常见错误 | Common Mistakes to Avoid When Starting a SideProject
Items.Description:
软件工程中的时间估计似乎是最困难的任务。

对于管理良好的项目来说，时间估算变得更容易一些，因为大多数设计决策已经做出并且有足够的内部工具和资源。 但它仍然不完美。 对于资源有限且截止日期有外部压力的团队来说，事情变得更加困难。 通常情况下，项目会交付，有些项目比其他项目更完善。

在时间估算方面，从事业余项目的独立开发人员面临着真正的挑战。 如果项目没有外部动力或收入潜力，情况会变得更糟。 构建一些有趣或学习的东西可能最终会成为一个从未有过发布日的未完成项目。
尽管不需要启动一个副项目，但它确实有助于结束并继续进行下一件大事。 如果你确实想完成你的下一个副项目并且找不到时间或动力，试着避免这些陷阱。

功能太多

副项目在设计上应该是直截了当的。 如果它有几十个微不足道的和非微不足道的功能，它正在成为一家初创公司。 想想你构建项目的原因。 如果是为了好玩，那么选择前三到五个功能包含在第一个版本中并继续前进。

无委派

即使您独自工作，也可以将自己视为一种资源。 问问自己每周工作几个小时可以交付多少，并为自己分配可完成的任务。 不要忘记在您的站点之外寻找可用于处理某些工作的工具和服务。

没有最后期限

一旦你清楚地知道你要构建什么，就为自己设定一个软硬期限。 给自己足够的缓冲来迎头赶上。

不必要的营销

不要陷入在编码之前进行无关的市场研究和用户调查的陷阱。 作为开发人员，您的优势在于开发。 创建可用于生产的产品比登陆页面更容易推向市场。

忙碌的工作

过多的计划会破坏流程，给你一种虚假的成就感。 在开始编码之前，不要创建一个广泛的任务列表和路线图。 一旦你完成了最初的几个功能并估计了它需要多少时间，就开始着手你的项目。 您可以随时修改估算并在此过程中删除或添加其他功能。

发射日的负担

不要太担心发射。 您不必到处展示您的发布和发布链接。 保持简单并确保您的项目最终在可访问的平台上结束。 您可以稍后共享指向您的项目的链接。

总之，如果您是出于娱乐或学习目的而从事辅助项目的开发人员，那么最好继续前进并以完成项目为目标并避免这些陷阱。



原文:

Time estimation in software engineering seems to be the most difficult task.

Time estimation becomes a bit easier for well-managed projects where most of the design decisions have been made and there is enough internal tooling and resources. But it is still not perfect. Things become more difficult for teams with limited resources and external pressure on deadlines. More often than not, the projects get delivered, some more polished than others.

When it comes to time estimation, solo developers working on their side projects face a real challenge. It becomes worse if there is no external motivation or revenue potential for the project. Building something for fun or learning might end up in an unfinished project that never sees a launch day.
Although launching a side project is not necessary, it does help in closure and moving on to the next big thing. If you do want to finish your next side project and cannot find the time or motivation, try avoiding these pitfalls.

Too Many Features

Side projects should be straightforward by design. If it has dozens of trivial and non-trivial features, it is on its way to becoming a startup. Think about the reason you are building your project. If it is for fun, then pick the top three to five features to include in the first version and move ahead.

No Delegation

Think of yourself as a resource even when you are working alone. Ask yourself how much you can deliver while working a few hours a week and assign achievable tasks to yourself. Don't forget to look for tools and services outside of your site that you can use to handle some of the work.

No Deadline

Set a soft and hard deadline for yourself once you have a clear idea of what you are trying to build. Give yourself enough buffer to catch up.

Unnecessary Marketing

Don’t fall into the trap of doing irrelevant market research and user surveys before coding. As a developer, your strength lies in development. Creating a production-ready product would be easier to market than a landing page.

Busy Work

Too much planning will ruin the flow and give you a false sense of accomplishment. Don’t create an extensive task list and roadmap before you start coding. Once you have your first few features finalized and an estimation of how much time it will take, start working on your project. You can always revise the estimate and drop or add another feature along the way.

Burden of Launch Day

Don’t worry too much about the launch. You don’t have to showcase your launch and post links everywhere. Keep it simple and ensure that your project ends up on an accessible platform. You can share the links to your project later.

In conclusion, if you are a developer working on a side project for fun or learning purposes, Then it's better to jump ahead and target finishing the project and avoid these pitfalls.
Items.Author: ranuzz

Items.Title: 最好的分布式 KV 数据库？ | Best distributed KV database?
Items.Description:
什么是最好的分布式键值数据库？ 我想根据用户体验来选择数据库，而不是通过认为他们的数据库是最好的网站。
数据库必须满足以下要求：

  • 易于设置
  • 可扩展到数百 TeraBytes

格雷西格雷戈里（她/她） - May 20 5月20日



原文:

What is the best distributed key-value database? I want to choose a database by user experience, not by the website that thinks their database is the best.
The database must meet these requirements:

  • Easy to setup
  • A HTTP API or a NodeJS / Javascript driver
  • Scalable to hundreds of TeraBytes

Gracie Gregory (she/her) - May 20

Items.Title: 5 个你必须使用的 VSCode 扩展 | 5 VSCode Extensions You Must Use
Items.Description:
这里有 5 个最有用的 Visual Studio Code 扩展来改进您的工作流程！

  • 误差镜头
  • 吉特镜头
  • 进口成本
  • 版本镜头

它还用不同的颜色突出显示线，以帮助您更好地可视化和区分错误、警告和其他消息！

有了这个扩展，您可以使用 html 文件中的 CSS 类搜索相关的 CSS 文件，并在那里自己编辑 CSS 文件！ 以下是它的工作原理：

所有这些信息都与您的光标当前所在的行内联。

您还可以将鼠标悬停在消息上以获取有关特定代码行的更多信息！

这些是 5 个强大、有用的 VSCode 扩展，可以提高您的工作效率和工作流程！

签字。

推特| 领英



原文:

Here are the 5 most useful Visual Studio Code extensions to improve your workflow!

  • Error Lens
  • CSS Peek
  • GitLens
  • Import Cost
  • Version Lens

1. Error Lens

Error Lens is an extension which allows you to see error, warning and diagnostic messages in line with your code without having to hover or click on anything! It also highlights the line with different colors to help you better visualize and differentiate between errors, warnings and other messages!

2. CSS Peek

With this extension, you can search for relevant CSS files by using CSS classes in html file and edit CSS files there itself! Here's how it works:

3. GitLens

GitLens is a powerful extension which can display when a particular line was committed, by whom it was committed and in which pull request it was included along with the commit message! All of this information is inline with the line upon which your cursor is currently located.

You can also hover over the message to get even more information about a particular line of code!

There are many more features in GitLens which you can explore and play around with!

4. Import Cost

Import Cost allows us to see the size of the imported package inline in the editor itself!

5. Version Lens

Version Lens fetches information about dependency versions and displays it on top of the package!

These were the 5 VSCode extensions which are powerful, useful and can improve your productivity and workflow!

Signing off.

Twitter | GitHub | LinkedIn

Items.Description: 原文:
Items.Author: fkafka007

Items.Title: Scrape ResearchGate 所有作者，Python 研究人员 | Scrape ResearchGate All Authors, Researchers in Python
Items.Description:
• 链接

先决条件

使用 CSS 选择器抓取基础知识

如果您还没有使用 CSS 选择器进行抓取，我有一篇专门的博客文章介绍了如何在网络抓取时使用 CSS 选择器，其中涵盖了它是什么、利弊，以及为什么从网络抓取的角度来看它们很重要和展示在网页抓取时使用 CSS 选择器的最常见方法。

独立的虚拟环境

如果您之前没有使用过虚拟环境，请查看使用 Virtualenv 和 Poetry 博客文章的专用 Python 虚拟环境教程以熟悉一下。

减少被阻止的机会

请求可能会被阻止。 看看如何减少网页抓取时被阻止的机会，有十一种方法可以绕过大多数网站的阻止。

使用代理抓取大量数据是一种方法。

安装库：

 pip install parsel playwright

完整代码

from parsel import Selector from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright import json def scrape_researchgate_profile ( query : str ): with sync_playwright () as p : browser = p . chromium . launch ( headless = True , slow_mo = 50 ) page = browser . new_page ( user_agent = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.64 Safari/537.36" ) authors = [] page_num = 1 while True : page . goto ( f "https://www.researchgate.net/search/researcher?q= { query } &page= { page_num } " ) selector = Selector ( text = page . content ()) for author in selector . css ( ".nova-legacy-c-card__body--spacing-inherit" ): name = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__title a::text" ). get () thumbnail = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__image img::attr(src)" ). get () profile_page = f 'https://www.researchgate.net/ { author . css ( "a.nova-legacy-c-button::attr(href)" ). get () } ' institution = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(3) span::text" ). get () department = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(4) span" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () skills = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(5) span" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). getall () last_publication = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::text" ). get () last_publication_link = f 'https://www.researchgate.net { author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::attr(href)" ). get () } ' authors . append ({ "name" : name , "profile_page" : profile_page , "institution" : institution , "department" : department , "thumbnail" : thumbnail , "last_publication" : { "title" : last_publication , "link" : last_publication_link }, "skills" : skills , }) print ( f "Extracting Page: { page_num } " ) # checks if next page arrow key is greyed out `attr(rel)` (inactive) -> breaks out of the loop if selector . css ( ".nova-legacy-c-button-group__item:nth-child(9) a::attr(rel)" ). get (): break else : # paginate to the next page page_num += 1 print ( json . dumps ( authors , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False )) browser . close () scrape_researchgate_profile ( query = "coffee" )

代码说明

导入库：

 from parsel import Selector from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright import json
代码解释
parsel 解析 HTML/XML 文档。 支持 XPath。
playwright 使用浏览器实例呈现页面。
json 将 Python 字典转换为 JSON 字符串。

定义一个函数并用上下文管理器打开一个playwright ::

 def scrape_researchgate_profile ( query : str ): with sync_playwright () as p : # ...
代码解释
query: str 告诉 Python query应该是一个str 。

午餐浏览器实例，使用传递user-agent打开new_page ：

 browser = p . chromium . launch ( headless = True , slow_mo = 50 ) page = browser . new_page ( user_agent = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.64 Safari/537.36" )
代码解释
p.chromium.launch() 启动 Chromium 浏览器实例。
headless 明确告诉playwright以无头模式运行，即使它是默认值。
slow_mo 告诉playwright放慢执行速度。
browser.new_page() 打开新页面。 如果不使用，它将默认为playwright值，即None 。 检查你的用户代理是什么。

添加一个临时列表，设置一个 while 循环，然后打开一个新 URL：

 authors = [] while True : page . goto ( f "https://www.researchgate.net/search/researcher?q= { query } &page= { page_num } " ) selector = Selector ( text = page . content ()) # ...
代码解释
goto() 使用传递的查询和页面参数向特定 URL 发出请求。
Selector() 使用page.content()传递返回的 HTML 数据并处理它。

遍历每一页上的作者结果，提取数据append加到一个临时list ：

 for author in selector . css ( ".nova-legacy-c-card__body--spacing-inherit" ): name = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__title a::text" ). get () thumbnail = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__image img::attr(src)" ). get () profile_page = f 'https://www.researchgate.net/ { author . css ( "a.nova-legacy-c-button::attr(href)" ). get () } ' institution = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(3) span::text" ). get () department = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(4) span" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). get () skills = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(5) span" ). xpath ( "normalize-space()" ). getall () last_publication = author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::text" ). get () last_publication_link = f 'https://www.researchgate.net { author . css ( ".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::attr(href)" ). get () } ' authors . append ({ "name" : name , "profile_page" : profile_page , "institution" : institution , "department" : department , "thumbnail" : thumbnail , "last_publication" : { "title" : last_publication , "link" : last_publication_link }, "skills" : skills , })

检查下一页是否存在并分页：

 # checks if the next page arrow key is greyed out `attr(rel)` (inactive) -> breaks out of the loop if selector . css ( ".nova-legacy-c-button-group__item:nth-child(9) a::attr(rel)" ). get (): break else : page_num += 1

打印提取的数据，并close浏览器实例：

 print ( json . dumps ( authors , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False )) browser . close () # call the function scrape_researchgate_profile ( query = "coffee" )

 [ { "name" : "Marina Ramón-Gonçalves" , # first profile "profile_page" : "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Marina-Ramon-Goncalves?_sg=VbWMth8Ia1hDG-6tFnNUWm4c8t6xlBHy2Ac-2PdZeBK6CS3nym5PM5OeoSzha90f2B6hpuoyBMwm24U" , "institution" : "Centro Nacional de Investigaciones Metalúrgicas (CENIM)" , "department" : "Reciclado de materiales" , "thumbnail" : "https://i1.rgstatic.net/ii/profile.image/845010970898442-1578477723875_Q64/Marina-Ramon-Goncalves.jpg" , "last_publication" : { "title" : "Extraction of polyphenols and synthesis of new activated carbon from spent coffe..." , "link" : "https://www.researchgate.netpublication/337577823_Extraction_of_polyphenols_and_synthesis_of_new_activated_carbon_from_spent_coffee_grounds?_sg=2y4OuZz32W46AWcUGmwYbW05QFj3zkS1QR_MVxvKwqJG-abFPLF6cIuaJAO_Mn5juJZWkfEgdBwnA5Q" }, "skills" : [ "Polyphenols" , "Coffee" , "Extraction" , "Antioxidant Activity" , "Chromatography" ] }, ... other profiles { "name" : "Kingsten Okka" , # last profile "profile_page" : "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Kingsten-Okka?_sg=l1w_rzLrAUCRFtoo3Nh2-ZDAaG2t0NX5IHiSV5TF2eOsDdlP8oSuHnGglAm5tU6OFME9wgfyAd-Rnhs" , "institution" : "University of Southern Queensland " , "department" : "School of Agricultural, Computational and Environmental Sciences" , "thumbnail" : "https://i1.rgstatic.net/ii/profile.image/584138105032704-1516280785714_Q64/Kingsten-Okka.jpg" , "last_publication" : { "title" : null , "link" : "https://www.researchgate.netNone" }, "skills" : [ "Agricultural Entomology" , "Coffee" ] }

原文:

  • What will be scraped
  • Prerequisites
  • Full Code
  • Links

Prerequisites

Basic knowledge scraping with CSS selectors

If you haven't scraped with CSS selectors, there's a dedicated blog post of mine about how to use CSS selectors when web-scraping that covers what it is, pros and cons, and why they're matter from a web-scraping perspective and show the most common approaches of using CSS selectors when web scraping.

Separate virtual environment

If you didn't work with a virtual environment before, have a look at the dedicated Python virtual environments tutorial using Virtualenv and Poetry blog post of mine to get familiar.

Reduce the chance of being blocked

There's a chance that a request might be blocked. Have a look at how to reduce the chance of being blocked while web-scraping, there are eleven methods to bypass blocks from most websites.

📌Note: if you'll be using this code snippet without proxies, the requests will be blocked in a short period of time. Scraping with proxies large amount of data is a way to go.

Install libraries:

pip install parsel playwright

Full Code

from parsel import Selector
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
import json def scrape_researchgate_profile(query: str): with sync_playwright() as p: browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True, slow_mo=50) page = browser.new_page(user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.64 Safari/537.36") authors = [] page_num = 1 while True: page.goto(f"https://www.researchgate.net/search/researcher?q={query}&page={page_num}") selector = Selector(text=page.content()) for author in selector.css(".nova-legacy-c-card__body--spacing-inherit"): name = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__title a::text").get() thumbnail = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__image img::attr(src)").get() profile_page = f'https://www.researchgate.net/{author.css("a.nova-legacy-c-button::attr(href)").get()}' institution = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(3) span::text").get() department = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(4) span").xpath("normalize-space()").get() skills = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(5) span").xpath("normalize-space()").getall() last_publication = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::text").get() last_publication_link = f'https://www.researchgate.net{author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::attr(href)").get()}' authors.append({ "name": name, "profile_page": profile_page, "institution": institution, "department": department, "thumbnail": thumbnail, "last_publication": { "title": last_publication, "link": last_publication_link }, "skills": skills, }) print(f"Extracting Page: {page_num}") # checks if next page arrow key is greyed out `attr(rel)` (inactive) -> breaks out of the loop
 if selector.css(".nova-legacy-c-button-group__item:nth-child(9) a::attr(rel)").get(): break else: # paginate to the next page
 page_num += 1 print(json.dumps(authors, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) browser.close() scrape_researchgate_profile(query="coffee")

Code explanation

Import libraries:

from parsel import Selector
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
import json
Code Explanation
parsel to parse HTML/XML documents. Supports XPath.
playwright to render the page with a browser instance.
json to convert Python dictionary to JSON string.

Define a function and open a playwright with a context manager::

def scrape_researchgate_profile(query: str): with sync_playwright() as p: # ...
Code Explanation
query: str to tell Python that query should be an str.

Lunch a browser instance, open new_page with passed user-agent:

browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True, slow_mo=50)
page = browser.new_page(user_agent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/101.0.4951.64 Safari/537.36")
Code Explanation
p.chromium.launch() to launch Chromium browser instance.
headless to explicitly tell playwright to run in headless mode even though it's a defaut value.
slow_mo to tell playwright to slow down execution.
browser.new_page() to open new page. user_agent is used to act a real user makes a request from the browser. If not used, it will default to playwright value which is None. Check what's your user-agent.

Add a temporary list, set up a while loop, and open a new URL:

authors = [] while True: page.goto(f"https://www.researchgate.net/search/researcher?q={query}&page={page_num}") selector = Selector(text=page.content()) # ...
Code Explanation
goto() to make a request to specific URL with passed query and page parameters.
Selector() to pass returned HTML data with page.content() and process it.

Iterate over author results on each page, extract the data and append to a temporary list:

for author in selector.css(".nova-legacy-c-card__body--spacing-inherit"): name = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__title a::text").get() thumbnail = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__image img::attr(src)").get() profile_page = f'https://www.researchgate.net/{author.css("a.nova-legacy-c-button::attr(href)").get()}' institution = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(3) span::text").get() department = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(4) span").xpath("normalize-space()").get() skills = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__stack-item:nth-child(5) span").xpath("normalize-space()").getall() last_publication = author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::text").get() last_publication_link = f'https://www.researchgate.net{author.css(".nova-legacy-v-person-item__info-section-list-item .nova-legacy-e-link--theme-bare::attr(href)").get()}' authors.append({ "name": name, "profile_page": profile_page, "institution": institution, "department": department, "thumbnail": thumbnail, "last_publication": { "title": last_publication, "link": last_publication_link }, "skills": skills, })

Check if the next page is present and paginate:

# checks if the next page arrow key is greyed out `attr(rel)` (inactive) -> breaks out of the loop
if selector.css(".nova-legacy-c-button-group__item:nth-child(9) a::attr(rel)").get(): break
else: page_num += 1

Print extracted data, and close browser instance:

print(json.dumps(authors, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) browser.close() # call the function
scrape_researchgate_profile(query="coffee")

Part of the JSON output:

[ { "name": "Marina Ramón-Gonçalves", # first profile "profile_page": "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Marina-Ramon-Goncalves?_sg=VbWMth8Ia1hDG-6tFnNUWm4c8t6xlBHy2Ac-2PdZeBK6CS3nym5PM5OeoSzha90f2B6hpuoyBMwm24U", "institution": "Centro Nacional de Investigaciones Metalúrgicas (CENIM)", "department": "Reciclado de materiales", "thumbnail": "https://i1.rgstatic.net/ii/profile.image/845010970898442-1578477723875_Q64/Marina-Ramon-Goncalves.jpg", "last_publication": { "title": "Extraction of polyphenols and synthesis of new activated carbon from spent coffe...", "link": "https://www.researchgate.netpublication/337577823_Extraction_of_polyphenols_and_synthesis_of_new_activated_carbon_from_spent_coffee_grounds?_sg=2y4OuZz32W46AWcUGmwYbW05QFj3zkS1QR_MVxvKwqJG-abFPLF6cIuaJAO_Mn5juJZWkfEgdBwnA5Q" }, "skills": [ "Polyphenols", "Coffee", "Extraction", "Antioxidant Activity", "Chromatography" ] }, ... other profiles { "name": "Kingsten Okka", # last profile "profile_page": "https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Kingsten-Okka?_sg=l1w_rzLrAUCRFtoo3Nh2-ZDAaG2t0NX5IHiSV5TF2eOsDdlP8oSuHnGglAm5tU6OFME9wgfyAd-Rnhs", "institution": "University of Southern Queensland ", "department": "School of Agricultural, Computational and Environmental Sciences", "thumbnail": "https://i1.rgstatic.net/ii/profile.image/584138105032704-1516280785714_Q64/Kingsten-Okka.jpg", "last_publication": { "title": null, "link": "https://www.researchgate.netNone" }, "skills": [ "Agricultural Entomology", "Coffee" ] }
]

Items.Title: DevOps 监控到底是什么？ | What exactly is DevOps Monitoring?
Items.Description:
顾名思义，DevOps 是指运维（Dev）和开发（Dev）以及运维（Ops）团队的组合，其基础是建立在 IT 自动化之上。 创新对每个企业都至关重要，因此他们继续开发新软件，以改善客户群并提高公司内部流程的效率。 然而，软件开发并不是一个安全的冒险，它很容易失败、失去资源以及开发和运营团队之间的高度紧张。

每个 IT 行业都面临着。 对于具有技术和非技术背景的人来说，DevOps 是一个很受欢迎的选择。 但让我们关注非技术人员从从事 DevOps 职业中受益的潜力。 为了提高您的 DevOps 能力，建议完成DevOps 研究生课程。

它需要对其生产环境中的应用程序、服务和基础架构的状态进行全面且最新的概述。 实时流媒体和过去回放以及可视化等功能是必不可少的服务和应用程序监控组件。

此外，它允许团队“向左移动”到开发的早期阶段，并减少生产错误机会的影响。 一个例子是更好地使用可以手动检测和响应任何错误的软件，通过随叫随到，并在可能的情况下自动检测。

如果您将循环的左侧部分视为产品侧，而将右侧部分视为操作侧，则负责将新功能推入生产的经理想知道项目如何分解为用户故事和任务。

项目左侧的开发人员必须弄清楚如何将该功能投入生产，包括项目票证、用户故事和依赖项。 如果开发人员遵循“您创建它，您管理它”的 DevOps 概念原则，他们也会对事件管理感兴趣。

转向生命周期的运营方面，现场的可靠性工程师必须知道可以监控和测量的功能，以便在出现问题时可以解决。

如果您没有链接各种流程的 DevOps 工具链，那么您将面临一个不干净、不相关和混乱的环境。 如果您有一个集成良好的工具链，您将对正在发生的事情有更深入的了解。



原文:

The name suggests that DevOps refers to the combination of operations (Dev) and development (Dev) as well as the operations (Ops) teams, and its foundation is built on IT automation. DevOps is an agile process that's a collection of techniques that eliminate manual labor to design stages, deploy, and test applications.

Innovation is vital for every business, so they continue to develop new software that could improve the customer base and improve the efficiency of processes within the company. However, software development isn't a safe venture that is susceptible to failure, losing resources and very high tensions between the development and the operations team.

Every IT industry faces. The advent of DevOps results in increased output and more productive working conditions for those businesses that adopt the method.

DevOps is a well-liked option for people who have technical and non-technical backgrounds. But let's focus on the potential for non-technical people to benefit from pursuing an occupation in DevOps. To increase your DevOps capabilities, it is recommended to complete the DevOps post graduate programs.

DevOps monitoring involves overseeing the whole development process, from planning to development integration and testing deployment and operations.

It requires a comprehensive and current overview of the state of the applications, services, and infrastructure within their production environments. Features like live streaming and replays of the past and visualizations are essential service and application monitoring components.

DevOps monitoring lets teams respond to any deterioration in customer service rapidly and efficiently. Additionally, it permits groups to "shift to the left" to earlier stages of development and reduce the impact of the chance of production errors. One example is the better use of software that can detect and react to any errors, by hand, through on-call, and automatically when possible.

DevOps monitoring vs. observability

If you look at the left-hand portion of the loop as the product side and the right-hand side as the operational side, the manager responsible for pushing a new feature into production would like to know how the project is broken down into user stories and tasks.

The developer on the left of the project has to figure out how to get the feature into production, including project tickets, user stories, and dependencies. If the developers follow the principles of the DevOps concept of "you create it, you manage it," They are also interested in incident management.

Moving on to the operations aspect of the lifecycle, the reliability engineer for the site must know the functions that can be monitored and measured so that should there be a problem, it can be addressed.

If you do not have a DevOps toolchain that links the various processes, you're left with an unclean, unrelated, and chaotic environment. If you've got a well-integrated toolchain, you will have greater insight into what's happening.

Items.Title: Ionic 4 角度路由器 + NavigationExtras 接口 | Ionic 4 angular router + NavigationExtras interface
Items.Description:
在 Ionic 5/angular 中的组件之间传递数据的示例：
使用 NavigationExtras 接口

尤其是在对话框中传递数据时，通常没有父/子关系。 所以你不能以角度方式传递数据（父->子）。 在 Ionic 4/angular 中，您可以通过NavigationExtras接口在路由器内轻松传递数据。

参考：第4点
萨尔玛·阿拉姆-奈勒 - May 12 5月12日



原文:

Example to pass data between components in Ionic 5/angular:
using NavigationExtras interface

Especially when passing data in dialogs there is often no parent/child-relation ship. So you can't pass data in angular way (parent -> child). In Ionic 4/angular you have this feature to pass data comfortable within the router by the interface NavigationExtras.

page1 pass data to page2

page1.ts

import {NavigationExtras, Router} from '@angular/router';
import {Component} from '@angular/core';

@Component({
    selector: 'page1',
    templateUrl: 'page1.html',
    styleUrls: ['page1.scss'],
})
export class Page1 {
    public data: string;
    public value: string;

    constructor(private router: Router) {
    }
    // route page2 and set params in NavigationExtras
    public routePage2() {
        const navigationExtras: NavigationExtras = {
            state: {
                data: this.data,
                value: this.value
            }
        };
        this.router.navigate(['/page2'], navigationExtras);
    }
}

page2.ts

import {ActivatedRoute, Router} from '@angular/router';
import {Component} from '@angular/core';
@Component({
    selector: 'page2',
    templateUrl: 'page2.html',
    styleUrls: ['page2.scss'],
})
export class Page2 {
    public data: string;
    public value: string;

    constructor(private route: ActivatedRoute, private router: Router) {
        this.route.queryParams.subscribe(params => {
            if (this.router.getCurrentNavigation().extras.state) {
                this.data = this.router.getCurrentNavigation().extras.state.data;
                this.value = this.router.getCurrentNavigation().extras.state.value;
            }
        });
    }
}

refer to: point 4
https://ngrefs.com/router/navigationextras
https://angular.io/api/router/NavigationExtras

Salma Alam-Naylor - May 12

Items.Title: AI 如何通过快速自动响应来防止网络安全威胁 | How AI prevents Cybersecurity Threats with Quick Automatic Response
Items.Description:
人工智能和机器学习几乎在每个行业都有用例，网络安全也不例外。

人们现在越来越多地在网络安全中使用人工智能来保护他们的数据和系统免受潜在的网络安全攻击。

在网络安全中使用人工智能有什么好处？

让我们详细讨论这些。

人工智能在网络安全中有很多用途，但下面提到的是前五名。 浏览此列表以了解在网络安全中使用 AI 的好处，并将 AI 集成到您的组织中。

人工智能在网络安全中最常见的用途是检测安全威胁。

虽然传统软件在一定程度上有助于检测恶意活动，但在网络安全中使用人工智能的作用远不止于此。

人工智能擅长理解模式并检测传统系统经常忽略的最微小的威胁。 借助 AI，您可以获得全面的网络安全解决方案，可以检测各种方式的网络安全威胁。

人工智能不仅用于威胁检测，还用于漏洞风险预测。

与传统系统不同，人工智能驱动的网络安全软件甚至可以在潜在威胁发生之前预测它们。

例如，如果您通过电子邮件或邮寄方式发送文件以进行签名，人工智能可以将其识别为潜在风险。 它会提醒您，您可以使用电子签名工具（例如DocuSign 替代品）来保护您的文档。

人工智能可以处理大量数据，并可以对 IT 资产进行盘点，以预测哪些区域未来容易受到攻击或安全漏洞。 您可以在网络安全中使用 AI 来识别漏洞并分配更多资源来专注于这些领域。

专业提示：通过使用安全的托管服务和良好的网站构建器来降低您网站的安全风险。

很大一部分在线流量由机器人组成，这可能会导致大量网络安全风险。

我们都知道有好的人工智能聊天机器人和坏的人工智能机器人。

搜索引擎爬虫等优秀的机器人对于 SEO 来说是必要的。 然而，不良机器人可能会造成很多威胁，例如创建虚假帐户和窃取用户的登录凭据。

它们对广告商尤其不利，因为它们可能会通过不相关的广告点击在 Google 上的广告上浪费金钱。

鉴于在线机器人流量的庞大，几乎不可能手动或使用传统系统跟踪这些自动化威胁。

这就是在网络安全中使用人工智能可以提供帮助的地方。 人工智能驱动的系统可以处理大量数据并区分两种类型的机器人。

通过这种方式，它可以识别包含恶意机器人的潜在风险网络流量，并防止它们造成任何损害。

如前所述，人工智能驱动的网络安全系统可以检测威胁并预测潜在的违规行为。

他们还能做什么？

它们还可以帮助您根据威胁可能造成的损害程度确定威胁的优先级，并更有策略地管理您的事件响应。

这意味着您将知道哪些事件是您的首要任务，需要立即关注，哪些事件可以在以后根除。

在网络安全中使用 AI 还可以帮助您减少事件响应时间并改进您的网络安全计划。 继续上一点，在网络安全中使用人工智能不仅可以提高您的响应时间，还可以使整个过程自动化。

人工智能驱动的网络安全系统可以全天候工作，自动检测和响应安全威胁。 虽然一些主要威胁需要人为干预，但人工智能可以应对数千个较小的日常网络安全风险。

因此，人工智能可以应对大量低优先级、低风险的威胁，而您的网络安全团队可以处理更高级的威胁。

这种智能自动化不仅可以响应威胁，还可以收集有关安全事件的数据并从中汲取见解。 因此，它也可以让您更好地应对未来的安全威胁。

专业提示：对其他流程使用自动化，例如自动化 Salesforce或其他 CRM，以简化流程并提高生产力。

准备好在网络安全中使用 AI 了吗？

虽然这些只是在网络安全中使用 AI 的五个主要好处，但还有更多理由让您张开双臂拥抱 AI。

在网络安全中使用 AI 可以节省大量时间，因为它可以处理从威胁检测到缓解的几乎所有网络安全方面。 让 AI 完成大部分繁重的工作，而您将注意力集中在最重要的任务上。

如果您还没有在网络安全中使用 AI，那么您应该这样做并亲自查看结果。

您对如何在网络安全中使用 AI 有任何疑问吗？ 随时在下面发表评论或开始讨论。



原文:

AI and machine learning have use cases in practically every industry and cybersecurity is no exception.

People are now increasingly using AI in cybersecurity to protect their data and systems from potential cybersecurity attacks.

What are the benefits of using AI in cybersecurity?

Let’s discuss these in detail.

5 Uses of AI in Cybersecurity

There are many uses of AI in cybersecurity, but the ones mentioned below are the top five. Go through this list to understand the benefits of using AI in cybersecurity and integrate AI into your organization as well.

1. Threat Detection

The most common use of AI in cybersecurity is to detect security threats.

While traditional software helps detect malicious activities to a certain extent, the use of AI in cybersecurity does more than that.

AI is adept at understanding patterns and detecting even the most minute threats that traditional systems often overlook. With AI, you can get a full-proof cybersecurity solution that can detect all manners of cybersecurity threats.

2. Breach Risk Prediction

AI is not just used for threat detection, but also for breach risk prediction.

Unlike traditional systems, AI-powered cybersecurity software can predict potential threats before they even occur.

For example, if you send your documents over emails or through the post to get them signed, AI could identify that as a potential risk. It will alert you and you can use electronic signature tools such as DocuSign alternatives to secure your documents.

AI can process tons of data and can take IT assets inventory to predict which areas are prone to future attacks or security breaches. You can use AI in cybersecurity to identify vulnerabilities and allocate more resources to focus on those areas.

Pro Tip: Reduce the security risks to your website by using a secure hosting service and a good website builder.

3. Bot Management

A huge portion of online traffic is comprised of bots, which can cause a whole lot of cybersecurity risks.

We all know that there are good AI enabled chatbots and bad AI enabled bots.

Good bots like search engine crawlers are necessary for SEO. Bad bots, however, can cause a lot of menace like creating bogus accounts and stealing users’ login credentials.

They’re especially bad for advertisers as they can cause a waste of money on ads on Google via irrelevant ad clicks.

Given the sheer amount of bot traffic online, it becomes almost impossible to keep track of these automated threats manually or using traditional systems.

That is where using AI in cybersecurity can help. AI-powered systems can go through large amounts of data and distinguish between the two types of bots.

AI can also understand customer journeys, the intent behind website traffic, and other behavioral patterns. This way, it can identify potentially risky web traffic comprising of bad bots and can keep them from doing any damage.

4. Incident Response Management

As discussed earlier, AI-powered cybersecurity systems can detect threats and also predict potential breaches.

What else can they do?

They can also help you prioritize threats based on the level of damage they can cause and manage your incident responses more strategically.

This means you’ll know which incidents are your top priority and need immediate attention and which ones can be rooted out later.

Using AI in cybersecurity also helps you reduce your incident response times and improve your cybersecurity program. AI allows you to quickly respond to threats and mitigate them before they can do any real damage.

5. Security Automation

Continuing with the previous point, the use of AI in cybersecurity can not only improve your response time but can automate the entire process.

AI-powered cybersecurity systems can work round-the-clock to detect and respond to security threats automatically. While some major threats would require human interference, AI can take care of thousands of smaller, day-to-day cybersecurity risks.

So, AI can respond to the high volume of low-priority, low-risk threats while your cybersecurity team can deal with the more advanced threats.

This intelligent automation does not just work to respond to threats, but also collects data about security incidents and draws insights from these. So, it sets you up to be better prepared for future security threats as well.

Pro Tip: Use automation for other processes like automating Salesforce or other CRM to streamline processes and improve productivity.

Ready to Use AI in Cybersecurity?

While these are just five key benefits of using AI in cybersecurity, there are a lot more reasons why you should embrace AI with open arms.

Using AI in cybersecurity frees up a lot of your time, as it can take care of almost all the aspects of cybersecurity from threat detection to mitigation. Let AI do most of the grunt work while you focus your attention on the most important tasks.

If you’re not yet using AI in cybersecurity, it’s high time that you do that and see the results yourself.

Do you have any questions on how to use AI in cybersecurity? Feel free to leave a comment below or start a discussion.
Items.Author: raytechguy

Items.Title: 停止将 AWS 凭证放入凭证文件中 | Stop putting AWS credentials in the credentials file
Items.Description:
对我来说，一切都是从这个 Twitter 线程和 Ben Kehoe 的那篇文章开始的：

在处理 CLI 或 SDK 时，我们已经习惯将 IAM 凭证放在.aws/credentials文件中。

但是，正如正确说明的那样，将凭据文件与许多凭据混为一谈并不是正确的做法。

本文旨在概述 IAM 委托人是什么以及管理您对 AWS 的编程访问的更好方法。

此外，我们将专注于证书流程。 我们将看到我们可以用它来完善我们对 AWS Principals 的本地使用。

让我们通过 AWS 授权系统深入了解这一旅程。

让我们从一些基础知识开始：IAM 校长！

说明官方文档： “委托人是可以请求对 AWS 账户或资源执行操作或操作的人或应用程序。”

由于遵循最佳实践的根账户不应用于编程访问，并且IAM 用户不是管理 AWS 身份的动态方式，因此提供商正在推动用户使用IAM 角色。

通过使用 AWS 角色，实体还可以通过假设角色技术获得另一个现有 AWS 账户的权限。

你得到了你的校长。 怎么办？ 访问其他帐户！

这是一篇全面的文章，介绍了如何访问其他 AWS 账户！

好的，我们已经了解了如何在最常见的场景中使用 Principals。

我们还知道，公司通常会将租户或简单地将工作负载划分到不同的帐户中，以保持隔离并简化治理和管理。

在现代 AWS 环境中，AWS 的建议是使用AWS Organizations 。

由亚马逊网络服务提供

要清楚了解的一件重要事情是AWS SSO 不是委托人！ 签署对 AWS 的 API 请求

您可以将 AWS 凭证放入凭证或配置文件中。 但是您知道 AWS 如何有效地授权使用这些凭证提出的请求者吗？ 使用名为Signature V4的过程。

访问密钥 ID、秘密访问密钥和会话令牌用于签署您对 AWS 服务发出的 HTTP 请求，验证访问服务的委托人。

他们永远不会被包含在请求中。 相反，您使用它为请求创建加密签名。

虽然 SigV4 嵌入在所有 AWS 开发工具包中，但它不会被单独使用（例如， API 网关需要对无法通过开发工具包发出的请求进行 SigV4 签名）。

凭据文件是纯文本文件，通常位于~/.aws/文件夹中。 它们可以是长期存在的（AWS IAM 用户）或短期存在的（AWS IAM 角色）。

凭证格式如下：

 [ default] aws_access_key_id = <YOUR_ACCESS_KEY_ID> aws_secret_access_key = <YOUR_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY> aws_session_token = <AWS_SESSION_TOKEN> #short-lived only

它们与 IAM 委托人直接关联。

这种方法有什么注意事项？ 让我们描述一些：

管理起来会很痛苦，因为我们现在知道 AWS SSO 不是委托人，因此不能直接使用它，需要一种方法来获取其角色以生成临时凭证。

虽然这本身不是问题，但 AWS CLI 目前并没有提供一种简单的方法来查看与特定 SSO 用户关联的角色，并且开发工具包需要自定义代码才能获得可用的凭证。

因此，总的来说，AWS SSO 仍然需要大量手动干预才能有效地用于编程访问。

通过手动或通过第三方工具设置，凭证文件中的临时凭证无法自动轮换。 在某些特殊情况下，这可能会导致严重困难：

  • 长时间运行的进程：如果您启动一个长时间运行的第三方进程，临时凭证将沿途过期，从而在任务中产生异常。 处理过期是凭证管理器的责任。
  • 用于自动轮换的第三方工具：在不中断外部流程的情况下处理轮换具有挑战性。

凭证文件的一般混乱

正如 Ben 在他的文章中正确指出的那样，凭证文件通常成为长期和短期凭证和不同配置文件的列表。

此列表不安全且难以管理。

是否有更好的方法来管理对服务和账户的所有 AWS 访问？ 是的，这称为凭证流程

凭证过程处理 AWS 凭证，以允许第三方工具在 AWS CLI、API 或 SDK 请求时生成凭证。

通过将命名的配置文件配置集放在位于~/.aws/config的配置文件中，格式如下：

 [ profile PROFILE_NAME] credential_process = PROCESS_GENERATING_CREDENTIAL_PATH_SCRIPT region = REGION

凭证过程需要外部工具以以下格式返回有效凭证：

 { "Version" : 1, "AccessKeyId" : "an AWS access key" , "SecretAccessKey" : "your AWS secret access key" , "SessionToken" : "the AWS session token for temporary credentials" , "Expiration" : "ISO8601 timestamp when the credentials expire" }

设置过期时间变量允许 AWS 准确知道何时再次请求凭证，而不会中断需要 AWS 的外部流程。

为什么使用凭证过程比使用凭证文件更好？ 凭证自动轮换！

在凭证文件中使用临时凭证的主要缺点之一是没有简单而智能的方法来刷新过期凭证，而且不会影响长时间运行的过程或基本操作。

还有另一个显着的好处：在您的机器、文件或环境变量中没有写入任何凭据。

即使认证过程是要走的路，仍然有一些重要的因素需要考虑：

  • 凭证流程凭证生成器：这意味着您使用的工具可以处理不同的 IAM 委托人。
  • 缓存凭据：由于外部工具需要生成凭据，因此还需要有效地缓存凭据。
  • 混乱：避免一长串配置规则。

这就是 OSS 项目Leapp可以成为您的杰作的地方。 结合桌面应用程序，配置文件本身可以由应用程序管理：

结论

在本文中，我们经历了漫长的旅程，了解 IAM 委托人的所有方面，以及它们如何用于以编程方式访问 AWS 提供的大量服务。

我们已经了解了 AWS SSO 如何与 AWS Organizations 一起使用，以及为什么不能将其误认为是委托人。 但我们也看到了 AWS Organizations 相对于常规跨账户访问的所有优势。

还解释了 Signature V4，提示 AWS 如何授权来自 CLI 和 SDK 的请求。

我们已经了解凭证文件和凭证流程之间的区别，以及为什么后者现在是长期运行流程和严格安全合规性的首选解决方案。

最后，我们讨论了我们的开源工具 Leapp，展示了它如何帮助处理凭证流程。

有问题吗？ 想提出解决方案？ 需要特别要求吗？ 在我的 Twitter 上留言或加入我们的slack社区并分享您的想法。

这就是所有人，直到下一篇文章，再见并保持安全😷。

*This article was written in pair with my co-worker @alessandro Gaggia ( @balubor ) *这篇文章是和我的同事@alessandro Gaggia ( @balubor ) 一起写的



原文:

To me, Everything started with this Twitter thread and from that article, by Ben Kehoe:

When dealing with CLI or SDKs, we are already used to putting IAM credentials in the .aws/credentials file.

However, as correctly stated cluttering the credentials file with many credentials is not the right move to make.

This article aims to overview what IAM Principals are and the better ways to manage your programmatic access to AWS.

Moreover, we'll focus on the Credential Process. We'll see that we can polish our local usage of AWS Principals with it.

Let's dig into this journey through AWS authorization systems.

Let's start with some basics: IAM Principals!

Stating the official documentation: "A principal is a person or application that can request an action or operation on an AWS account or resource."

IAM Principals can either be the User Root Account or an IAM entity (User or Role).

Because Root Account, following best practices, should not be used for programmatic access, and IAM Users are not a dynamic way to manage AWS identities, the provider is pushing users to use IAM Roles.

AWS Roles can be used to grant permissions to external Identity providers (Okta, OneLogin, AzureAd, and more..) to assign them to SSO Identities.

By using AWS Roles, an entity can also obtain permission in another existing AWS account via the assumeRole technique.

You obtained your Principal. Now what? Gain access to other Accounts!

One of the best things about IAM Roles is that they can be "assumed" by letting other entities (Users, Services, Applications) act on behalf of that role.

Here is a comprehensive article that explains how you can gain access to other AWS accounts!

AWS SSO: it's an authentication process, not a Principal!

Ok, we have seen how to use Principals in the most common scenarios.

We also know that it is common for companies to divide their tenants or simply their workloads into different accounts to maintain isolation and simplify governance and management.

In a modern AWS environment, AWS advice is to use AWS Organizations.

Courtesy of Amazon Web Services

AWS Organizations allows for AWS Single Sign-On, which is the ability to authenticate a valid external Identity, into the AWS ecosystem, through a Federation Process.

One important thing to understand clearly is that AWS SSO is not a Principal! AWS SSO allows for authentication inside AWS by external meanings, but it does not handle authorization against AWS services.

AWS associates one or more IAM Roles to an authenticated user via AWS SSO to manage authorization.

Signing API requests to AWS

You can put AWS credentials inside credentials or config files. But do you know how AWS effectively authorizes requesters made with those credentials? With a process called Signature V4.

Access key id, secret access key, and session token are used to sign the HTTP requests you make to AWS services, verifying the principal accessing the service.

They never get included in the request. Instead, you use it to create a cryptographic signature for the request.

While SigV4 is embedded in all the AWS SDKs, it is not exposed to being used independently (e.g., API Gateway requires SigV4 signatures on requests that can't be made through the SDK).

AWS Credentials file and temporary credentials

A credentials file is a plain text file, located typically in the ~/.aws/ folder. They can be long-lived (AWS IAM User) or short-lived (AWS IAM Role).

The credentials format is the following:

[default]
aws_access_key_id = <YOUR_ACCESS_KEY_ID>
aws_secret_access_key =<YOUR_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY>
aws_session_token=<AWS_SESSION_TOKEN> #short-lived only

They are used directly (with a specific profile or with a default one) by AWS CLI and SDKs.

They are directly associated with an IAM Principal.

What are the caveats of such an approach? Let's describe some:

AWS SSO

It can be a pain to manage as we now know that AWS SSO is not a principal, so it cannot be used directly and needs a way to obtain its role to generate temporary credentials.

While this is not a problem per se, AWS CLI currently does not give an easy way to view which role is associated with a specific SSO user, and SDKs require custom code to obtain usable credentials.

So, in general, AWS SSO still needs much manual intervention to be used effectively for programmatic access.

Short-lived credentials used in the credentials file can't be auto-rotated by AWS

Being set either manually or via third-party tools, temporary credentials inside the credentials file can't be rotated automatically. Ben Kehoe stated in his article that good practice should require the opposite.

There are some particular scenarios in which this can cause serious difficulties:

  • Long-running processes: if you start a long-running third-party process, temporary credentials will expire along the way, generating exceptions in the task. It is a credential manager's responsibility to take care of the expiration.
  • Third-party tools for automatic rotation: it is challenging to handle rotation without disrupting external processes.

General cluttering of credential files

As correctly stated by Ben in his article, the credentials file usually becomes a list of long and short-lived credentials and different profiles.

This list is not secure and challenging to manage.

Is there a better way to manage all AWS access to services and accounts? Yes, it's called Credential Process

The credential process handles AWS credentials to allow a third-party tool to generate credentials when requested by AWS CLI, APIs, or SDKs.

By putting a named profile configuration set in the config file located in ~/.aws/config formatted like below:

[profile PROFILE_NAME]
credential_process=PROCESS_GENERATING_CREDENTIAL_PATH_SCRIPT
region=REGION

The credential_process variable tells AWS how to request valid temporary credentials when needed.

The credential process requires the external tool to return valid credentials in the format:

{ "Version": 1, "AccessKeyId": "an AWS access key", "SecretAccessKey": "your AWS secret access key", "SessionToken": "the AWS session token for temporary credentials", "Expiration": "ISO8601 timestamp when the credentials expire"
}

Setting the expiration time variable allows AWS to know precisely when requesting the credentials again without disrupting the external process requiring AWS.

Why is it better to use the credential process than the credentials file? Credentials auto-rotation!

One of the major drawbacks of using temporary credentials in the credentials file is that there is no simple and intelligent way to refresh expired credentials, not without compromising a long-running process or essential operations.

There is another significant benefit: no credentials are written on your machine, in files, or environment variables.

Even if the credential process is the way to go, there are still some important factors to consider:

  • Credential Process credentials generator: this implies that the tool you're using can handle diverse IAM Principals.
  • Caching credentials: because credentials generation is demanded by an external tool, caching credentials efficiently is also requested.
  • Cluttering: avoid a long list of config rules.

Here is where the OSS project Leapp can be your handiwork. Leapp can manage all major IAM Principals and AWS SSO, converting all of them into viable temporary credentials.

Leapp is a valid credential process for AWS:

[profile PROFILE_NAME]
credential_process=leapp session generate SESSION_ID
region=REGION

In combination with the Desktop App, the config file itself can be managed by the App:

credential-process.mp4

Conclusions

In this article, we have come down a long journey through all the aspects of IAM Principals and how they are used to gain programmatic access to the plethora of services that AWS offers.

We have seen how AWS SSO works with AWS Organizations and why it must not be mistaken for a Principal. But we have also seen all the advantages of AWS Organizations over regular cross-account access.

Signature V4 was also explained, giving hints on how AWS authorizes requests coming from CLI and SDKs.

We have understood the differences between credentials file and credentials process and why the latter is nowadays a much-preferred solution for long-running processes and strict security compliances.

Finally, we have discussed Leapp, our open-source tool, showing how it can help deal with the credentials process.

Have questions? Want to propose a solution? Need to request something particular? Drop a line on my Twitter or come and join us in our slack community and share your thoughts.

So that's all folks, till the next article, goodbye and stay safe 😷.

*This article was written in pair with my co-worker @alessandro Gaggia (@balubor)

Items.Title: PostgreSQL：使用 WITH 子句分解问题的示例 | PostgreSQL: An example of using WITH clauses to decompose the problem
Items.Description:
这是一个使用 WITH 子句（又名 CTE - 通用表表达式）来分解问题的简单示例。 我的建议是以下查询：

 with input_month as ( select 4 as month --> the input months (can be a parameter in a prepared statement) ), day_generator as ( select date_trunc ( 'year' , now ()) --> I guess you query for the current year + m . month * interval '1 month' --> first day of the month + n * interval '1 day' --> adding 31 days to cover any month as day from input_month m , generate_series ( 0 , 31 ) n ), days_of_month as ( select * from input_month m , day_generator d where d . day < --> removes the days in next month date_trunc ( 'year' , now ()) + ( m . month + 1 ) * interval '1 month' ), result as ( select distinct d . day , r . roomNo from days_of_month d , BookedRooms b join Rooms r on ( b . roomId = r . PK ) where not ( d . day between b . checkInDate and b . checkOutDate ) --> remove non available rooms order by day , roomno ) select * from result

我首先将输入参数定义为input_month 。 它可以作为参数传递，但我发现在查询开始时只将它放在一个地方而不是在多个地方引用$1更具可读性

然后我需要生成一个月的所有日子，我分两步完成。 并且days_of_month过滤器仅获取星期一的天数（对于少于 31 天的月份）。

最后，查询是日期和预订之间的笛卡尔连接，过滤日期间隔以仅显示可用的。 我添加了一个 DISTINCT 以防有重叠，但这不应该发生，应该用约束检查。 这可能是下一篇博文的主题。

我将最终查询放在result中，以便主选择是最简单的选择，这样可以通过选择每个步骤来轻松调试。

我发现分解成 CTE 有很多优点：

  • 更易于阅读和评论每个步骤
  • 查询计划器更容易处理（实现或不实现每个查询块的可能性）
  • 更容易测试，只需更改最终选择 只要设计良好，不要害怕大型 SQL 查询。 您可以将其想象为一个接一个地通过管道传输 Linux 命令以通过小步骤解决问题。

乔凡妮·巴博萨—— Mar 22 3月22日



原文:

Here is a simple example using WITH clauses (aka CTE - Common Table Expressions) to decompose a problem. A question from the YugabyteDB forum was about storing hotel room bookings in a table like:


create table Rooms (PK int primary key, name text, roomNo int); create table BookedRooms (PK int primary key
, checkInDate timestamptz, checkOutDate timestamptz
, roomId int references Rooms(PK))
; insert into Rooms values (2,'Deluxe',102),(3,'King',103);
insert into BookedRooms values (1,'2022-05-26T00:00:00Z','2022-05-29T00:00:00Z',2),(2,'2022-05-29T00:00:00Z','2022-05-30T00:00:00Z',3);

My suggestion was the following query:

with input_month as ( select 4 as month --> the input months (can be a parameter in a prepared statement)
),
day_generator as (
select date_trunc('year', now()) --> I guess you query for the current year + m.month * interval '1 month' --> first day of the month + n * interval '1 day' --> adding 31 days to cover any month as day from input_month m , generate_series(0,31) n
),
days_of_month as (
select *
from input_month m, day_generator d where d.day < --> removes the days in next month date_trunc('year', now()) + (m.month + 1) * interval '1 month' ),
result as (
select distinct d.day, r.roomNo from days_of_month d, BookedRooms b join Rooms r on (b.roomId=r.PK)
where not(d.day between b.checkInDate and b.checkOutDate) --> remove non available rooms
order by day, roomno
) select * from result

I start by defining the input parameters as input_month. It can be passed as parameter, but I find it more readable to have it at only one place, in the start of the query, rather than referenceing $1in multiple places

Then I need to generate all days for a month and I do it in two steps. day_geneator generates the maximum (31 days a months) starting from the input_month. And days_of_month filters to get only the days of the mons (for months with less than 31 days).

Finally, with all that, the query is a cartesian join between the dates and the bookings, filtering on the date interval to show only those available. I've added a DISTINCT in case there are overlaps, but this should not happen and should be checked with constraints. This may be a topic for a next blog post.

I put the final query in result so that the main select is the simplest one, this makes it easy to debug by selecting each step.

I find many advantages in decomposing into CTE:

  • easier to read and comment each step
  • easier to process by the query planner (possibility to materialize or not each query block)
  • easier to test, just by changing the final select Do not fear large SQL queries as long as they are well designed. You can think of it like piping Linux commands one after the other to resolve a problem with small steps.

Giovanne Barbosa - Mar 22

Patente C: Esame patente C a proposito di funziona ?

L’ricerca che sfilata della patente C, consiste con un questionario a proposito di 40 domande, il quale il aspirante dovrà porre fine nell’ temporale 30 minuti.

Le domande tra l’studio, verranno estrapolate con 25 temi spettatori un database, al anelante intorno a regnare la atto a loro sarà concessa una benignità di quattro errori.

Ricordatevi, il quale nato da tenere d'occhio la in modo migliore sui quiz patente, l’incartamento deve individuo continuo.

Items.Title: 使用多个 API 可视化全球分布的团队 | Visualizing a Globally Distributed Team Using Multiple APIs
Items.Description:
如果说 2020 年的大流行教会了我们什么，那么远程优先的工作文化就是未来的发展方向。 作为 Postman 的新开发人员倡导者，我很高兴能够远程为分布在全球的开发人员关系团队工作，该团队支持 Postman 在全球拥有超过 2000 万用户的社区。 话虽如此，我受到启发建立了我的第一个系列，以突出 Postman 的全球影响力。

在Postman 30 天的第 17天，我对Postman Visualizer功能产生了兴趣。 我知道它将成为我项目的核心，所以我开始寻找一种方法来可视化我遍布全球的出色的新 Postman 团队成员。 在这篇博文中，我将展示我是如何开始的。

在脑海中处理图像

世界各地都有 Postmanauts，我的团队由 7 名成员组成，他们跨越不同的时区工作。 地图将成为展示我们全球多样性的最佳可视化方式。 在探索了Postman API Network之后，我偶然发现了地震地图可视化，它使用D3.js库将数据映射到经度和纬度坐标。


世界地震地图

我意识到只要我能够访问一些经度和纬度数据，我就可以在这张地图上可视化任何东西。

是时候检验这个理论了。 我使用Postman Echo API构建了一个名为Random Users的示例 POST 请求。 这个请求背后的想法是使用 Postman 的faker 库为 10 个具有随机名称、经度和纬度的用户回显一些样本虚拟数据。

 let users = []; for (var i=0; i<10; i++){ let tempUsers = { name: pm.variables.replaceIn("{{$randomFullName}}"), latitude: pm.variables.replaceIn("{{$randomLatitude}}"), longitude: pm.variables.replaceIn("{{$randomLongitude}}") } users.push(tempUsers); } pm.collectionVariables.set('req_body', JSON.stringify(users));

在这个预请求脚本中，我使用动态变量为十个具有随机名称、纬度和经度的用户创建对象。 然后将这些对象推入一个数组并使用集合变量和JSON 序列化设置到请求正文中。 发送此请求后，我可以获得随机用户：


包含来自 Postman Echo Post Request 的随机用户的响应

现在是可视化的时候了。 在测试选项卡中，我使用了地震示例中的模板并修改了几个有趣的点：

 var response = pm.response.json().data; let parsedData = []; //data parsing for (let users of response){ let tempEntry = {}; tempEntry.name = users.name; tempEntry.lat = users.latitude; tempEntry.long = users.longitude; tempEntry.circleSize = 6; tempEntry.color = "#F09D51"; parsedData.push(tempEntry); } // visualize pm.visualizer.set(template, { data: parsedData, title: "Map of Random Users" } );

然后使用pm.visualizer.set()方法将解析后的数据可视化，地图的标题从 echo API 返回。 这是我们得到的：


随机用户的可视化

瞧，理论奏效了！ 这证明有了一些经度和纬度坐标，我可以使用这个可视化工具来映射随机用户。 这将是我的可视化模板，但现在是时候处理一些真实的用户数据了。

选择 API

为真实用户获取数据可能很棘手。 我需要为我的整个团队找到经度和纬度数据，所以我想从他们的 Twitter 个人资料中使用他们的位置数据作为起点。

为了从用户名中获取用户数据，我专门使用Twitter API user by username端点来检索单个用户名的位置数据，并使用Twitter API User by Usernames从多个用户名中检索位置数据。 在 Postman 中，user.fields 的查询参数用于从 Twitter 获取各种数据，例如：

 Created_at,description,entities,id,location,name,pinned_tweet_id,profile_image_url,protected,url,username,verified,withheld

这个请求是从Twitter API v2 Postman Collection派生出来的，它允许任何人通过Twitter 的 Developer Dashboard的正确授权访问大量的 Twitter 端点。

就我而言，我使用我的 Twitter 句柄@poojamakes来获取我的位置数据，即华盛顿特区


一旦我可以使用他们的个人资料为每个团队成员获取高级位置，我需要一个地理编码 API，它可以根据他们的位置为我提供他们的经度和纬度。

要使用此 API，您需要从 PositionStack 获取 API 密钥，方法是单击 获取免费 API 密钥并选择免费选项供个人使用。

在这种情况下，我使用转发地理编码端点发出转发地理编码请求，使用 API 的forward端点并使用来自 Twitter 的位置指定query参数。

现在我可以使用 Twitter API 访问位置，并且能够使用 PositionStack API 将该位置转换为经度和纬度数据，我正在顺利创建我的地图可视化。

我决定有点创意，并使用Open Weather API使用Current Weather Data端点捕获每个团队成员的天气数据，它允许您获取地球上任何位置的当前天气数据，包括超过 200,000 个城市，通过使用地理坐标（纬度，经度）。

在这一点上，我有所有的拼图：

现在是时候构建一个单一的工作流程（或者可能很多）来将所有这些部分组合在一起。

构建工作流

我在做这个项目时学到的一件事是，使用 Postman 构建请求工作流有很多不同的方法。 就我而言，我发现了三种具体方法，我将在即将发布的博文中深入探讨。 但是，这些工作流背后的共同点是能够使用序列化跨每个请求创建、设置和解析数据对象。 我的同事Ian Douglas写了一篇关于这个主题的精彩博客文章，其中谈到了“序列化”数据以在读写内存时存储和转换复杂数据类型的想法。

对于这种情况，我将此数据存储到对象环境变量中。 在处理多个 API 和请求时，通常需要变量。 在这个场景中，我使用一个环境变量来设置我将在 Twitter API 中使用的所有 Twitter 用户名，以获取每个用户的位置数据和名称。 然后，我能够使用 pm.environment.set() 使用该数据创建一个对象，并使用pm.environment.set() JSON.stringify()序列化数据设置为一个字符串，并将其设置为另一个环境变量，以用于 PositionStack API 的查询参数获取每个用户的经度和纬度。

 let response = pm.response.json(); // building users object let obj= { name : response.data.name, location:response.data.location, latitude: null, longitude: null } pm.environment.set("user-object", JSON.stringify(obj));

最初，此数据设置为 null，但随着数据通过另一个 API 并使用JSON.parse()进行反序列化和解析，可以将越来越多的数据添加到每个请求中。

 var dataOut = pm.response.json().data[0]; let lat = dataOut.latitude; let long = dataOut.longitude; //add long and lat from object var userObj = JSON.parse(pm.environment.get("user-object")); userObj.latitude = lat userObj.longitude = long // set lat and long to main object pm.environment.set("user-object", JSON.stringify(userObj));

我们从 API 的响应中获取经度和纬度数据。 然后解析初始环境变量并将数据添加到对象中，最后再次设置为使用pm.environment.set()以在另一个请求中使用。

使用这些方法作为每个场景的模型为我们提供了整个工作流程：

  1. 从 Twitter API 获取用户的位置。
  2. 将位置转换为经度和纬度。
  3. 使用经度和纬度根据每个用户的位置获取天气数据。
  4. 可视化地图中的所有数据。

这是此工作流程中所有内容的可视化概览。 最终的 POST 请求使用 Postman Echo API，它将随机数据替换为来自 Twitter、PositionStack 和 Open Weather API 的每个用户的真实数据：


创建您自己的团队可视化

总的来说，这个系列的创建是为了展示 Postman 多功能性的魔力。 仅仅了解使用变量、对象、序列化和可视化的基础知识就可以让您走得更远。

请留意我即将发布的关于构建 Postman 工作流的三种方法的博文，在这篇博文中，我将使用同一个示例深入探讨处理单个 Postman 请求、 从脚本发送请求以及构建请求工作流。

如果您有兴趣玩转这个收藏并在全球范围内想象您自己的队友，请务必分叉我的收藏。


映射的七个 Postman 开发人员关系队友是： @iandouglas736 、@ poojamakes 、@ DevRelSean 、@ arlemi 、@ jansche 、@ PetuniaGray 、@ DevRelKev

给我们所有人关注或使用此集合来查找您网络中的其他 Twitter 用户以进行可视化。

要开始使用此集合：

  1. 获取Twitter 不记名令牌并将bearer_token添加到集合变量中的当前值。
  2. 获取PositionStack API 密钥并将api-access-key添加到集合变量中的当前值。
  3. 获取Open Weather API 密钥并将weather_api_key添加到集合变量中的当前值。
  4. 使用收集运行器运行单个邮递员请求并将所有对象捕获为每个用户的环境变量。

查看Postmanauts - Visualize请求。

The post Visualizing a Globally Distributed Team Using Multiple APIs appeared first on Postman Blog . 使用多个 API 可视化全球分布式团队的帖子首先出现在Postman 博客上。



原文:

If the 2020 pandemic has taught us anything, remote-first work culture is the way of the future. As a new developer advocate at Postman, I’m excited to be working remotely for a globally distributed developer relations team supporting Postman’s community of more than 20 million users worldwide. With that said, I was inspired to build my first collection to highlight Postman’s global presence.

On Day 17 of 30 Days of Postman, I became intrigued with the Postman Visualizer feature. I knew it was going to be the heart of my project, so I set out to find a way to visualize my fantastic new Postman team members spanning the globe. In this blog post, I’ll show how I got started.

Working with an image in mind

There are Postmanauts living in all corners of the world, with my team comprising seven members that work across various time zones. A map was going to be the best kind of visualization to showcase our global diversity. After exploring the Postman API Network, I stumbled upon an Earthquake Map Visualization, which used D3.js libraries to map data against longitude and latitude coordinates.


Map of earthquakes around the world

I realized that I could visualize just about anything on this map as long as I had access to some longitude and latitude data.

Time to put this theory to the test. I built a sample POST request called Random Users using the Postman Echo API. The idea behind this request was to echo back some sample dummy data for ten users with random names, longitudes, and latitudes using Postman’s faker library.

let users = [];

for (var i=0; i<10; i++){
    let tempUsers = {
        name: pm.variables.replaceIn("{{$randomFullName}}"),
        latitude: pm.variables.replaceIn("{{$randomLatitude}}"),
        longitude: pm.variables.replaceIn("{{$randomLongitude}}")

    }
    users.push(tempUsers);
}
pm.collectionVariables.set('req_body', JSON.stringify(users));

In this pre-request script, I used dynamic variables to create objects for ten users with random names, latitudes, and longitudes. These objects are then pushed into an array and set into the request body using a collection variable and JSON Serialization. Once this request is sent, I can get random users:


Response that includes random users from Postman Echo Post Request

Now it’s time for the visualization. In the Test tab, I used the template from the Earthquake example and modified a couple of interesting points:

var response = pm.response.json().data;
let parsedData = [];

//data parsing
for (let users of response){
    let tempEntry = {};
    tempEntry.name = users.name;
    tempEntry.lat = users.latitude;
    tempEntry.long = users.longitude;
    tempEntry.circleSize = 6;
    tempEntry.color = "#F09D51";
    parsedData.push(tempEntry);
}
// visualize 
pm.visualizer.set(template, {
    data: parsedData,
    title: "Map of Random Users"
} );

The Echo API’s response data is being parsed for each of the users, name, latitude, longitude, and circle size and color for each data point. The pm.visualizer.set() method is then used to visualize the parsed data, and the map’s title is returned from echo API. Here is what we get:


Visualization of Random Users

Voilà, the theory worked! This proved that with some longitude and latitude coordinates, I could use this visualizer to map random users. This was going to be my template to visualize, but now it was time to work with some real user data.

Choosing the APIs

Getting data for real users can be tricky. I needed to find longitude and latitude data for my entire team, so I thought to use their location data from their Twitter profiles as a starting point.

The Twitter API is a really powerful API. To get a user’s data from a username, I specifically used the Twitter API user by username endpoint to retrieve location data for an individual username and the Twitter API User by Usernames to retrieve location data from multiple usernames. In Postman, the query params for user.fields is used to get all sorts of data from Twitter, such as:

Created_at,description,entities,id,location,name,pinned_tweet_id,profile_image_url,protected,url,username,verified,withheld

This request is forked from the Twitter API v2 Postman Collection, which allows anyone to access the wide plethora of Twitter endpoints with the right authorization from Twitter’s Developer Dashboard.

In my case, I used my Twitter handle, @poojamakes, to get my location data, which is Washington, D.C.


Twitter API V2 User by Username request

Once I could get a high-level location for each of my team members using their profiles, I needed a geocoding API that could give me their longitude and latitudes based on their location.

The PositionStack API was a perfect fit since it provided an accurate forward and reverse geocoding covering more than 2 billion places and addresses worldwide. To use this API, you will need to obtain an API key from PositionStack by clicking on Get Free API Key and selecting the free option for personal use.

In this scenario, I used the Forward Geocoding endpoint to make a forward geocoding request, using the API’s forward endpoint and specifying the query parameter with the location from Twitter.

API results:


Example of PositonStack Forward Geocoding endpoint

Now that I had the access to location using the Twitter API and was able to convert that location into longitude and latitude data using PositionStack API, I was well on my way to creating my map visualization.

I decided to get a little creative and also capture the weather data for each team member by using the Open Weather API using the Current Weather Data endpoint, which allows you to get current weather data for any location on Earth, including over 200,000 cities, by using geographical coordinates (lat, lon).

At this point I have all the pieces of the puzzle:

Now it’s time to build out a single workflow (or maybe many) to put all of these pieces together.

Building workflows

One thing I learned while working on this project was that there are so many different ways to build request workflows with Postman. In my case, I found three specific ways that I will be diving deeper into in my upcoming blog post. However, the common thread behind these workflows is the ability to create, set, and parse through data objects across each request using serialization. My colleague Ian Douglas wrote a wonderful blog post on this topic which talks about the idea of “serializing” data to store and convert complex data types when writing and reading from memory.

For this case, I am storing this data into object environment variables. Variables in general are necessary when working with multiple APIs and requests. In this scenario, I used an environment variable to set all of the Twitter usernames that I will be using in the Twitter API to get the location data and name for each user. I was then able to create an object with that data using pm.environment.set() and set that serialized data into a string, using JSON.stringify(), into another environment variable to be used in a query parameter for the PositionStack API to get each user’s longitude and latitude.

let response = pm.response.json();
// building users object 
let obj= { 
     name : response.data.name,
     location:response.data.location,
     latitude: null,
     longitude: null
}
pm.environment.set("user-object", JSON.stringify(obj));

Initially, this data is set as null, but as the data passes through another API and is deserialized and parsed using the JSON.parse(), more and more data can be added to each request.

var dataOut = pm.response.json().data[0];
let lat = dataOut.latitude;
let long = dataOut.longitude;

//add long and lat from object
var userObj = JSON.parse(pm.environment.get("user-object"));
userObj.latitude = lat
userObj.longitude = long

// set lat and long to main object
pm.environment.set("user-object", JSON.stringify(userObj));

We are getting the longitude and latitude data from the API’s response. Then the initial environment variable is parsed and the data is added to the object and finally set again to using pm.environment.set() to be used in another request.

Using these methods as a model for each scenario gives us the overall workflow:

  1. Get user’s location from Twitter API.
  2. Convert Location into longitude and latitude.
  3. Use longitude and latitude to get weather data for each user based on their location.
  4. Visualize all the data in the map.

Here’s a visual overview of everything in this workflow. The final POST request uses the Postman Echo API which replaces the random data with real data for each user from the Twitter, PositionStack, and Open Weather APIs:


API workflow

Create your own team visualizations

Overall, this collection was created to showcase the magic of Postman’s versatility. Just knowing the basics of working with variables, objects, serialization, and visualizations can take you a long way.

Keep an eye out for my upcoming blog post about the three ways to build Postman workflows, in which I’ll use this same example to dive deeper into working with individual Postman requests, sending requests from scripts, and building request workflows.

If you are interested in playing around with this collection and visualizing your own teammates across the globe, be sure to fork my collection.


The seven Postman developer relations teammates that are mapped are: @iandouglas736, @poojamakes, @DevRelSean, @arlemi, @jansche, @PetuniaGray, @DevRelKev

Give us all a follow or or use this collection to find other Twitter users in your network to visualize.

To get started with this collection:

  1. Fork this collection into your own workspace.
  2. Get Twitter bearer token and add bearer_token to current value in collection variable.
  3. Get PositionStack API key and add api-access-key to current value in the collection variable.
  4. Get Open Weather API key and add weather_api_key to current value in the collection variable.
  5. Use collection runner to run through Individual Postman Requests and capture all objects as environment variables for each user.

Check out visualization in Postmanauts - Visualize request in all folders.

The post Visualizing a Globally Distributed Team Using Multiple APIs appeared first on Postman Blog.

Items.Title: 为什么不应该在 .NET 中使用 Dynamic 类型 | Why you shouldn’t use the Dynamic type in .NET
Items.Description:
如果你是像我这样的爱好者，当微软发布一些关于语言的更新、新的库或任何关于什么是做“这个”的新奇特方式的文档时，你会感到兴奋。

但随着时间的推移，新的开发人员可能会质疑其中一些新增功能的相关性，并最终会支持或反对它们。 那么，Dynamic 和 ExpandoObject 已经过时了吗？ 我们应该使用它们吗？

为什么咆哮:)

这篇文章的想法来自我最近检查的一个 PR，我看到我的同事使用 Dynamic 和 ExpandoObject...（对不起 R**** ！）

什么是动态

简而言之，动态的使用告诉编译器跳过所有检查，如语法，而是在运行时评估它们：

dynamic test=new object();
test.Bark();

即使我们知道该对象不包含 Bark() 函数，这段代码也会正常编译。 此错误将改为在运行时显示。

问题

就像 Async 关键字一样，当你开始使用动态时，你就停不下来。 您的动态代码将像病毒一样扩展并到处冒泡，从而消除了 C# 最被低估的好处之一，即静态类型和编译时检查……

在某种程度上，您将开始在 C# 代码中编写 Python 或 JS。 如果您真的想这样做，请从一开始就使用其他语言！

接下来，可读性：您正在使用一个无法通过 IDE 跟踪的对象，如果即使编译器也会跳过对它的理解，您如何用您正在编写的代码表达您的意图。 甚至 C# 也不理解你在写什么，并且会即时评估它，所以我们应该怎么做;)

由于这还不够，性能：构建和编译阶段在执行之前节省了大量时间。 这是编译语言和解释语言之间的主要区别……
每个人都有他的优点和缺点，但是在云环境中，当你想从应用程序中榨取每一点性能时，你也应该考虑到这一点。

最后但同样重要的是，想想你的 bug 搜寻之旅，当一个新人改变对象或函数中的任何东西时。 使用此功能时您真的感到安全吗？ 即使你有单元测试，接受算法中的每一个可能的值不会吓到你吗？

那么它应该！

例外

如果是这样，微软为什么要在 2010 年推出它？！ 如前所述，每个功能都属于特定的上下文并解决问题。 所以回到过去，C# 是 Windows 独有的。 如果您还记得 .NET Framework（时光飞逝……），它曾是 Java 和其他语言的直接竞争对手。

借助动态类型功能，Microsoft 希望实现一种称为COM Interop的东西。 这个想法是您可以在 C# 和 .NET 中集成和运行各种应用程序和平台。 这有利于将现有系统和脚本集成到您的代码库中。

一个实际的例子是，您有一个现有的 Ruby 应用程序，并且您开始使用 C# 作为桌面应用程序。 您可以安装一个名为 IronRuby 的库并在 C# 中加载您的 Ruby 代码。 因此，如果您在 Ruby 中创建了一个类，您可以在您的 .NET 应用程序中实例化并使用它，这太疯狂了！

此功能实际上是为此目的而制作的。 现在有了我们新的便携式.NET。 大多数情况下，我们认为这不是很相关，并且被认为是一个利基功能。

一个实际的例外

但是只有一个小的实际场景我仍然使用它们……导航一个复杂的 JSON 对象。 老实说，这可以说是我认为这很合适的唯一背景。

如何不使用它

我是静态类型和编译时检查的忠实粉丝。 因此，如果我可以删除动态甚至拳击类型，我会发现这是理想的。

因此，作为一般规则和结论：

  • 尝试使用泛型，如果这不起作用：
  • 尝试使用对象和拳击，当且仅当它不起作用时：
  • 您现在唯一的选择是 Dynamic 类型和 ExpandoObject :)

但是，嘿，至少你试过了！

雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— May 23 5月23日



原文:

If you’re an enthusiast like myself, you will get excited when Microsoft release some sort of updates on the language, new libraries or any documentation on what is the new fancy way of doing ‘This’.

Microsoft target those releases to respond and enhance our development experience, thus each feature is the answer to a specific problem in our code. But as times goes by, new developers may question the relevance of some of these additions, and ultimately have a stand with or against them. So, is Dynamic and ExpandoObject obsolete already? Should we use them?

Why Rant :)

The idea for this article came from a PR I was recently checking, I saw my colleague using Dynamic and ExpandoObject… (Sorry R**** !)

What is Dynamic

Briefly, the usage of dynamic tell the compiler to skip all checks like syntaxes and evaluate them on runtime instead:

dynamic test=new object();
test.Bark();

This code will compile normally, even though we know that object does not contain a Bark() function. This error will be shown on runtime instead.

The Problem

Just like the Async keyword, when you start using dynamic, you can’t stop. Your dynamic code will expand just like a virus and bubble up everywhere, removing one of the most underrated benefits of C# which is static types and compile-time checking…

In a way, you will start writing Python or JS in your C# code. If you really want to do that, use that other language from the beginning !

Next, Readability: You’re using an object that you cannot track with your IDE, how can you express your intent with the code you’re writing if even the compiler will skip understanding it. Even C# does not understand what you’re writing and will evaluate it on the fly so how should we ;)

As it wasn’t enough, Performance: The building and compilation phase saves a lot of time ahead of execution. This is the main difference between compiled and interpreted languages…
Each one has his pros and cons but in a cloud environment when you want to squeeze every bit of performance from your app, you should also consider that.

Last but certainly not least, think about your bug hunting journeys, when a newcomer changes anything in the objects or functions. Do you really feel safe while using this feature? Even if you have unit tests, doesn’t accepting each and every possible value in your algorithm scare you?

Well it should !

The Exception

If this is the case, Why did Microsoft introduce it back in 2010 ?! As discussed before each feature belong to a specific context and solve a problem. So back in the time, C# was Windows exclusive. If you remember .NET Framework (Time flies…), it was a direct competitor to java and other languages.

With the dynamic type feature, Microsoft wanted to achieve something called COM Interop. The idea was that you can integrate and run diverse applications and platforms inside C# and .NET. This was good for integrating with existing systems and scripts into your codebase.

A practical example would be that you have an existing Ruby app and you started using C# for a Desktop App. You can install a library called IronRuby and load your Ruby code INSIDE C#. So if you created a class in Ruby, you can instantiate and use it in your .NET App, which was insane !

This feature was actually made for this purpose. Nowadays with our new and portable .NET. We don’t find this very relevant most of the time and is considered a niche feature.

An Actual Exception

But there’s only a small practical scenario where I still use them… Navigating a complex JSON object. Honestly this is arguably the only context where I see this fitting even.

How to not use it

I am a huge fan of static types and compile time checking. So if I can remove dynamics and even boxing types I would find that ideal.

So as a general rule and conclusion:

  • Try to use generics, if that doesn’t work:
  • Try to use objects and boxing, if and only if it doesn’t work:
  • Your only resort is now the Dynamic type and ExpandoObject :)

But hey, at least you tried !

Jakub Andrzejewski - May 23
