原文:

Introduction

The ZEGOCLOUD Express-Video SDK provides the ability to monitor the streaming quality during stream publishing and stream playing. After stream publishing or stream playing is started, the SDK triggers the corresponding streaming quality callback every 3 seconds, and client applications can obtain real-time streaming quality data by listening for these callbacks.

Prerequisites

Before you monitor the streaming information, make sure you complete the following:

Monitor the quality of stream publishing

To monitor the quality of the stream publishing, listen for the onPublisherQualityUpdate callback. After stream publishing is started, this callback will be triggered every 3 seconds to provide the stream publishing quality data.

You can obtain stream publishing quality data through this callback to monitor the health of stream publishing in real time and display the upstreaming network quality on your application's UI.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPublisherQualityUpdate(String streamID, ZegoPublishStreamQuality quality) { // You can focus on specific quality attributes and report them to your business server, or focus on a specific field of an object for giving friendly notes for users. // The parameter 'quality' has many attributes. If you do not want to handle every one of them, you can just focus on the 'level' attribute, a comprehensive quality indicator calculated by the SDK based on other quality attributes. Log.v("onPublisherQualityUpdate: streamID=", streamID); }
}

The stream publishing quality attributes in details

The stream publishing quality includes the audio and video capture frame rate, bitrate, RTT, packet loss rate, and others of capturing and encoding stages of the stream publishing process.

The ZegoPublishStreamQuality object includes quality attributes of different stages of the stream publishing process. You can find the detailed attribute definitions in this section:

Quality attributes of audio and video capturing

The following attributes reflect the stream publishing quality of audio and video capturing, which is close to the user's subjective perception of the local preview.

  • audioCaptureFPS: The audio capture frame rate (fps).
  • videoCaptureFPS: The video capture frame rate (fps).

Quality attributes of video encoding

The stream publishing quality attributes of video encoding of the encoding stage is as follow:

  • videoEncodeFPS: The target output frame rate (fps) of video encoding.

Quality attributes of stream transmission

The stream publishing quality attributes below reflect the quality of stream transmission (sending), which is related to the encoding bitrate and the current network quality.

  • audioSendFPS: The actual audio transmission frame rate (fps).
  • audioKBPS: The actual audio transmission bitrate (kbps).
  • videoSendFPS: The actual video transmission frame rate (fps).
  • videoKBPS: The actual video transmission bitrate (kbps).
  • rtt: The rount-trip time (ms) from the client device to the ZEGO server.
  • packetLostRate: The client-side upstreaming packet loss rate.

Quality attributes of the total number of bytes

The stream publishing quality attributes below reflect the total number of bytes sent.

  • totalSendBytes: The total number of bytes sent, including audio, video, SEI.
  • audioSendBytes: The number of audio bytes sent.
  • videoSendBytes: The number of video bytes sent.

Quality attributes of encoding information

The stream publishing quality attributes below reflect the encoding information:

  • videoCodecID: The video codec.
  • isHardwareEncoder: Whether to enable hardware encoding.

Quality attributes of published stream quality level

The parameter quality has many attributes. If you do not want to handle every one of them, you can just focus on the level attribute, a comprehensive upstreaming network qualityindicator calculated by the ZegoExpressEngine based on other quality attributes.

The following table describes the level fields:

Monitor the quality of stream playing

To monitor the quality of the stream playing, listen for the onPlayerQualityUpdate callback. After stream playing is started, this callback will be triggered every 3 seconds to provide the stream playing quality data.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPlayerQualityUpdate(String streamID, ZegoPlayStreamQuality quality) { // You can focus on specific quality attributes and report them to your business server, or focus on a specific field of an object for giving friendly notes for users. // The parameter 'quality' has many attributes. If you do not want to handle every one of them, you can just focus on the 'level' attribute, a comprehensive quality indicator calculated by the SDK based on other quality attributes. Log.v("onPlayerQualityUpdate: streamID=", streamID); }
}

The stream playing quality attributes in details

The stream playing quality includes the audio and video frame rate, bitrate, delay, packet loss rate, and others of receiving, decoding, and rendering stages of the stream playing process.

The ZegoPlayStreamQuality object includes quality attributes of different stages of the stream playing process. You can find the detailed attribute definitions in this section:

Quality attributes of stream receiving

The stream playing quality attributes below reflect the quality of stream receiving, which is related to the quality of stream transmission (sending) and the current network quality.

  • audioRecvFPS: The actual audio receiving frame rate (fps).
  • audioDejitterFPS: The audio dejitter frame rate (f/s).
  • audioKBPS: The actual audio receiving bitrate (kbps).
  • audioBreakRate: The actual received audio break rate (number of breaks / every 10 seconds).
  • videoRecvFPS: The actual video receiving frame rate (fps).
  • videoDejitterFPS: The video dejitter frame rate (f/s).
  • videoKBPS: The actual video receiving bitrate (kbps).
  • videoBreakRate: The actual received video break rate (number of breaks / every 10 seconds).
  • packetLostRate: The client-side downstreaming packet loss rate, in percentage, 0.0 - 1.0.
  • rtt: The round-trip time (ms) from the client device to the ZEGO server.
  • avTimestampDiff: The difference between the video timestamp and the audio timestamp, used to reflect the synchronization of audio and video, in milliseconds. This value is less than 0 means the number of milliseconds that the video leads the audio, greater than 0 means the number of milliseconds that the video lags the audio, and 0 means no difference. When the absolute value is less than 200, it can basically be regarded as synchronized audio and video, when the absolute value is greater than 200 for 10 consecutive seconds, it can be regarded as abnormal.
  • peerToPeerDelay: The delay from peer to peer, in milliseconds.
  • peerToPeerPacketLostRate: The packet loss rate from peer to peer, in percentage, 0.0 - 1.0.

Quality attributes of stream rendering

The stream playing attributes below reflect the streaming quality at the stream rendering stage, which is close to the viewer's subjective perception. They are affected by the audio/video codec, and the values may be lower than the actual audio/video receiving frame rates.

  • audioRenderFPS|_blank](@audioRenderFPS-ZegoPlayStreamQuality): The audio rendering frame rate (fps).
  • [videoRenderFPS: The video rendering frame rate (fps).
  • delay: The delay after the data is received by the local end, in milliseconds.

Quality attributes of the total number of bytes

The stream publishing quality attributes below reflect the total number of bytes received.

  • totalRecvBytes: The total number of bytes received, including audio, video, SEI.
  • audioRecvBytes: The number of audio bytes received.
  • videoRecvBytes: The number of video bytes received

Quality attributes of decoding information

The stream playing quality attributes below reflect the decoding information:

  • videoCodecID: The video codec.
  • isHardwareDecode: Whether to enable hardware decoding.

Monitor the status of stream publishing/playing

Callback for updates on stream publishing status

After stream publishing starts, if the status changes, the SDK sends out the event notification through the onPublisherStateUpdate callback. To monitor the status of stream publishing, listen for this callback.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPublisherStateUpdate(String streamID, ZegoPublisherState state, int errorCode, JSONObject extendedData) { super.onPublisherStateUpdate(streamID, state, errorCode, extendedData); // In this callback, when the [state] is [ZegoPublisherState.NO_PUBLISH] and the [errorCode] is not 0, indicates the stream publishing is failed. And the SDK won't retry to publish the stream. At this time, user can set a corresponding UI to indicate the current state.  // In this callback, when the [state] is [ZegoPublisherState.PUBLISH_REQUESTING] and the [errorCode] is not 0, indicates the stream publishing retry is ongoing. But if the stream publishing retry still fails after the default reconnection duration, it won't try again and will send out a failure notification. }
}

You can determine the user's network status during stream publishing based on the state property.

The following table describes the value of the state property and the corresponding user status:

The parameter extendedData provides extended information for status updates. If you are using the ZEGO CDN, the key of this parameter is flv_url_list, rtmp_url_list, and hls_url_list, which correspond to the stream playing URL of FLV, RTMP, and HLS protocols respectively.

Callback for updates on stream playing status

After stream playing starts, if the status changes, the SDK sends out the event notification through the onPlayerStateUpdate callback. To monitor the status of stream playing, listen for this callback.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPlayerStateUpdate(String streamID, ZegoPlayerState state, int errorCode, JSONObject extendedData) { super.onPlayerStateUpdate(streamID, state, errorCode, extendedData); // In this callback, when the [state] is [ZegoPlayerState.NO_PLAY] and the [errorCode] is not 0, indicates the stream playing is failed. And the SDK won't retry to play the stream. At this time, user can set a corresponding UI to indicate the current state.  // In this callback, when the [state] is [ZegoPlayerState.PLAY_REQUESTING] and the [errorCode] is not 0, indicates the stream playing retry is ongoing. But if the stream playing retry still fails after the default reconnection duration, it won't try again and will send out a failure notification. }
}

You can determine the user's network status during stream playing based on the state property.

The following table describes the value of the state property and the corresponding user status:

Monitor the first frame of audio and video

Callback on capturing the first frame of audio

To receive the event notification when the first frame of audio is captured, listen for the onPublisherCapturedAudioFirstFrame callback.

After stream publishing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the first frame of audio is captured.

When you enable stream publishing or local preview for the first time, the SDK engine starts to capture the audio data of the local device, and the SDK sends out event notification through this callback.
You can tell whether the audio data has been captured successfully according to the returned event notification. If no notifications are received, it means the audio device is being used or the device is abnormal.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPublisherCapturedAudioFirstFrame() { super.onPublisherCapturedAudioFirstFrame(); }
}

Callback on capturing the first frame of video

To receive the event notification when the first frame of video is captured, listen for the onPublisherCapturedVideoFirstFrame callback.

After stream publishing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the first frame of video is captured.

When you enable stream publishing or local preview for the first time, the SDK engine starts to capture the video data of the local device, and the SDK sends out event notification through this callback.
You can tell whether the video data has been captured successfully according to the returned event notification. If no notifications are received, it means the video device is being used or the device is abnormal.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPublisherCapturedVideoFirstFrame(ZegoPublishChannel channel) { super.onPublisherCapturedVideoFirstFrame(channel); }
}

Callback on receiving the first frame of audio

To receive the event notification when the first frame of audio is received, listen for the onPlayerRecvAudioFirstFrame callback.

After stream playing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the first frame of audio is received.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPlayerRecvAudioFirstFrame(String streamID) { super.onPlayerRecvAudioFirstFrame(streamID); AppLogger.getInstance().receiveCallback("onPlayerRecvAudioFirstFrame streamID:%s",streamID); }
}

Callback on receiving the first frame of video

To receive the event notification when the first frame of video is received, listen for the onPlayerRecvVideoFirstFrame callback.

After stream playing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the first frame of video is received.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPlayerRecvVideoFirstFrame(String streamID) { super.onPlayerRecvVideoFirstFrame(streamID); }
}

Callback on rendering the first frame of video

To receive the event notification when the first frame of video is rendered, listen for the onPlayerRenderVideoFirstFrame callback.

After stream playing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the first frame of video is received and has been rendered.

You can also use this callback to count the time consumed by capturing/receiving/rendering the first frame or use this callback to update the UI components of stream playing.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPlayerRenderVideoFirstFrame(String streamID){ super.onPlayerRenderVideoFirstFrame(streamID); }
}

Monitor the changes on video resolution

Callback for the changes on the captured video resolution

To receive the event notification when the captured video size changes, listen for the onPublisherVideoSizeChanged callback.

After stream publishing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the resolution of the captured video changes.

When you enable stream publishing or local preview for the first time, the SDK engine starts to capture the video data of the local device, and the resolution of the video changes simultaneously.

You can use this callback to remove the UI elements in the view of the local video preview, or adjust the scale of the view of the local preview dynamically based on the resolution returned by the event callback. 

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPublisherVideoSizeChanged(int width, int height, ZegoPublishChannel channel) { super.onPublisherVideoSizeChanged(width, height, channel); }
}

Callback for the changes on the played video resolution

To receive the event notification when the played video resolution changes, listen for the onPlayerVideoSizeChanged callback.

After stream playing starts, the SDK sends out the event notification through this callback when the resolution of the played video changes. You can adjust the video display based on the final resolution of the video streams.

  • You won't receive any notifications when playing audio-only streams.

  • If the stream publisher end triggers the traffic control within the SDK due to network issues, the code distinguishability on the stream publisher side may be dynamically reduced, and SDK sends out notification through this callback.

  • The SDK sends out notification when the UI of the streams you played is being rendered. You can use this callback to update or switch the UI elements.

engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPlayerVideoSizeChanged(String streamID, int width, int height) { }
}

Monitor the status of the stream forwarding via CDN

Callback for updates on the status of CDN URL

To receive event notification when the specified CDN URL changes (adds new URL or removes existing URL), listen for the onPublisherRelayCDNStateUpdate callback.

After the ZEGO RTC Server forwards the stream to CDN, the SDK sends out event notification when the status of streams forwarded to CDN changes, for example, the stream forwarding stops or the forwarding operation retries.

You can tell whether the audio and video streams forwarded to CDN are normal according to this callback:

  • If the returned result of this callback indicates it is abnormal, locate the cause of the audio and video streams forwarded to CDN and perform a disaster recovery operation accordingly.
  • If you do not know the cause of the exception, contact ZEGO technical support for further analysis.
engine.setEventHandler(new IZegoEventHandler() { @Override public void onPublisherRelayCDNStateUpdate(String streamID, ArrayList<ZegoStreamRelayCDNInfo> infoList) { super.onPublisherRelayCDNStateUpdate(streamID, infoList); }
}

The stream forwarding attributes in details

The stream forwarding information includes the CDN URL, forwarding status, the cause of stream forwarding status, and when the status changes.

The ZegoStreamRelayCDNInfo object includes stream forwarding attributes. You can find the detailed attribute definitions in this section:

The following table describes the value of the state property:

The following table describes the value of the updateReason property:

Items.Title: 如何选择适合您的 Web 开发包？ | How To Choose Which Web Development Package Is Right For You?
Items.Description:
系统逻辑软件解决方案

发表于关于网络网页介绍软件程序有很多选择，想要成为网络时尚裁缝师可能很难确定选择哪一个。 我们可以学习最大的著名网络介绍程序：Front Page、Dreamweaver 和 Adob​​e Go Live。 所有 3 都提供了一个令人愉快的界面和许多业余和高级替代方案，能够让您创建您梦想中的互联网站点。 绝大多数托管组都获得了这 3 个程序的最后输出，但是，您仍然希望测试您的网页与独一无二的网络浏览器的兼容性。 该界面就像您在 Word 或 Excel 中找到的界面一样，具有许多相同的按钮、键盘快捷键和操作。 文本格式在 FrontPage 中是小菜一碟，因为它几乎看起来就像您使用 Word 来完成它。 插入/图片命令也在这里，允许人们毫不费力地将快照组合到网页中。 尽管许多熟练的网络设计师将 FrontPage 视为主要的软件程序，但您仍然可以通过使用 HTML 视图的方式将您的网页调整到更好的要求。 您在使用 HTML 功能方面拥有更大的自由度，让您的网页朝着完美的方向发展。 一个非常有益的选择是布局和代码切割视图，您会注意到您在供应代码中所做的修改结果会实时影响布局。 您还可以测试最新推出的独一无二的浏览器，这是一个非常关键的步骤，可让您校准网页以获得最佳兼容性。 由于其 WYSIWIG（所见即所得）界面，它同样是一个适合新手的超级程序，这允许人们在没有 HTML 编码专业知识的情况下创建 .net 网站。 但是，一些 HTML 编码专业知识将允许您创建更高搜索的网站。 系统逻辑软件解决方案是设计师最喜欢的网络介绍软件程序，可用于运行不同的 Adob​​e 产品，包括 Photoshop 或 Illustrator。 熟悉的 Adob​​e 工作区减少了此应用程序的学习曲线，并允许跨软件程序因素的干净集成。 网页的照片元素通常在 Photoshop 中创建，是通过简单的拖放移动方式定位到 GoLive 工作区的效果。 与 Adob​​e 商品的不同照片布局的超级兼容性推荐 GoLive 作为您需要创建出色的搜索互联网站点时的绝佳选择。

Items.Title: Next.js 中的响应式布局与 Chakra-ui | Responsive layout in Next.js with Chakra-ui
Items.Description:
本周早些时候，我在 Next.js 中发布了一篇关于使用 Chakra-ui 的响应式和自定义组件的文章。

我想写一篇后续文章并专注于响应式布局，而Chakra-ui网格组件使这样做变得轻而易举。

我做了一个小演示，在一张类似个人资料的小卡片上显示一些朋友，建立在我之前文章中解释的内容之上


 //pages/index.js const Home = () => { const mainRef = useRef (); const dimensions = useDimensions ( mainRef , true ); const [ size , setSize ] = useState ( '' ); useEffect (() => { if ( dimensions ) { let width = dimensions . borderBox . width ; switch ( true ) { case width < 499 : setSize ( ' base ' ); break ; case width >= 499 && width < 696 : setSize ( ' sm ' ); break ; case width >= 696 && width < 945 : setSize ( ' md ' ); break ; case width >= 945 : setSize ( ' lg ' ); break ; } } }, [ dimensions ]); return ( <> < Nav /> < Box w = " 100vw " h = " 100vh " display = " flex " justifyContent = " center " ref = { mainRef } alignItems = " flex-start " as = " main " > < Layout > < MyGrid /> < Box fontSize = {{ base : ' 20px ' , sm : ' 24px ' }} mt = " 30px " > < Text display = " flex " justifyContent = " center " > Width : { dimensions && dimensions . borderBox . width } px < /Text > < Text display = " flex " justifyContent = " center " > Custom breakpoint size : { size } < /Text > < /Box > < /Layout > < /Box > < / > ); }; export default Home ;

在整篇文章中，您会注意到某些组件道具上的{{base: 'someValue', sm: 'someValue', ...}}语法，您可以在我的另一篇文章中看到我更深入地解释它，但是总结一下，它根据我们在主题中定义的自定义断点告诉道具是一个特定的值。

 //styles/ChakraTheme.js import { extendTheme } from ' @chakra-ui/react ' ; const theme = extendTheme ({ colors : { primary : ' #201D29 ' , }, breakpoints : { sm : ' 499px ' , md : ' 696px ' , lg : ' 945px ' , }, }); export default theme ;

最后，为了帮助我们可视化断点，我们可以利用来自 Chakra 的钩子来获取包装布局组件的 Box 的宽度并将其存储在 state 中，并且每次组件调整大小时它都会在 useEffect 中更新。

 import { useRef , useState , useEffect } from ' react ' ; import { Box , Text } from ' @chakra-ui/react ' ; import MyGrid from ' ../components/MyGrid ' ; import Layout from ' ../components/Layout ' ; import Nav from ' ../components/Nav ' ; import { useDimensions } from ' @chakra-ui/react ' ; const Home = () => { const mainRef = useRef (); const dimensions = useDimensions ( mainRef , true ); const [ size , setSize ] = useState ( '' ); useEffect (() => { if ( dimensions ) { let width = dimensions . borderBox . width ; switch ( true ) { case width < 499 : setSize ( ' base ' ); break ; case width >= 499 && width < 696 : setSize ( ' sm ' ); break ; case width >= 696 && width < 945 : setSize ( ' md ' ); break ; case width >= 945 : setSize ( ' lg ' ); break ; } } }, [ dimensions ]); return ( <> < Nav /> < Box w = " 100vw " h = " 100vh " display = " flex " justifyContent = " center " ref = { mainRef } alignItems = " flex-start " as = " main " > < Layout > < MyGrid /> < Box fontSize = {{ base : ' 20px ' , sm : ' 24px ' }} mt = " 30px " > < Text display = " flex " justifyContent = " center " > Width : { dimensions && dimensions . borderBox . width } px < /Text > < Text display = " flex " justifyContent = " center " > Custom breakpoint size : { size } < /Text > < /Box > < /Layout > < /Box > < / > ); }; export default Home ;

在额外加载时，大小状态是一个空字符串const [size, setSize] = useState('')并且将在第一次渲染到屏幕后在 useEffect 中更新。

每次来自Chakra的尺寸对象时，我们将使用 switch 语句来确定将尺寸状态更新为什么。

你可以在这里看到源代码
跟我来



原文:

Earlier this week I posted an article on responsive and custom components with Chakra-ui in next Next.js.

I wanted to write a follow up and focus on a responsive layout, and the Chakra-ui Grid Component makes it a breeze and pleasure to do so.

I've made a small demo, displaying some friends on a small profile-like card, building on top of what explained in my previous article



//pages/index.js const Home = () => { const mainRef = useRef(); const dimensions = useDimensions(mainRef, true); const [size, setSize] = useState(''); useEffect(() => { if (dimensions) { let width = dimensions.borderBox.width; switch (true) { case width < 499: setSize('base'); break; case width >= 499 && width < 696: setSize('sm'); break; case width >= 696 && width < 945: setSize('md'); break; case width >= 945: setSize('lg'); break; } } }, [dimensions]); return ( <> <Nav /> <Box w="100vw" h="100vh" display="flex" justifyContent="center" ref={mainRef} alignItems="flex-start" as="main" > <Layout> <MyGrid /> <Box fontSize={{ base: '20px', sm: '24px' }} mt="30px"> <Text display="flex" justifyContent="center"> Width: {dimensions && dimensions.borderBox.width}px </Text>
 <Text display="flex" justifyContent="center"> Custom breakpoint size: {size} </Text>
 </Box>
 </Layout>
 </Box>
 </>
 );
};
export default Home;

The index component returns a React Fragment containing a simple custom Nav component and a Layout container component to house our Grid component.


//components/MyGrid.js import profiles from '../profiles.json'; const MyGrid = () => { return ( <> <Grid templateColumns={{ base: '1fr', lg: 'repeat(2, 1fr)' }} h="100%" gap="10px" overflow="auto" css={{ '&::-webkit-scrollbar': { width: '4px', }, '&::-webkit-scrollbar-track': { width: '6px', }, '&::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb': { background: 'primary', borderRadius: '24px', }, }} > {profiles.map((profile) => ( <GridItem key={profile.id} h="150px" bg="primary" borderRadius="10" color="white" display="flex" pl={{ base: '10px', sm: '25px', lg: '15px' }} alignItems="center" > <Box display="flex" alignItems="center"> <ItemAvatar imageSrc={profile.imageSrc} name={profile.name} />
 </Box>
 <Details name={profile.name} title={profile.title} link={profile.linkSrc} />
 </GridItem>
 ))} </Grid>
 </>
 );
}; export default MyGrid;

The Grid component imports a json file from our root and we map over that data and for each object in that array, we return a Grid Item from Chakra, with an Avatar component from Chakra and our own custom Details component inside.


//components/Details.js import { Box, Text } from '@chakra-ui/react';
import Link from 'next/link'; const Details = ({ name, title, link }) => { return ( <Box display="flex" flexDirection="column" pl={{ base: '10px', sm: '25px', lg: '15px' }} fontSize={{ base: '12px', sm: '14px', md: '16px', lg: '14px' }} > <Text fontSize="20px"> {name} </Text>
 <Text as="i">{title}</Text>
 <Text color="#03fcfc" as="i"> <Link href={link}>{link}</Link>
 </Text>
 </Box>
 );
}; export default Details;

Throughout this article, you notice the {{base: 'someValue', sm: 'someValue', ...}} syntax on some of the components props and you can see me explain it in more depth in my other article, but to summarize, it tells the prop to be a certain value, based on the custom breakpoints we defined in the theme.


//styles/ChakraTheme.js import { extendTheme } from '@chakra-ui/react'; const theme = extendTheme({ colors: { primary: '#201D29', }, breakpoints: { sm: '499px', md: '696px', lg: '945px', },
}); export default theme;

Finally, to help us visualize our breakpoints, we can utilize a hook from Chakra to get the the width of our Box that wraps our Layout component and store it in state, and it will update in a useEffect every time on the component resize.


import { useRef, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { Box, Text } from '@chakra-ui/react';
import MyGrid from '../components/MyGrid';
import Layout from '../components/Layout';
import Nav from '../components/Nav';
import { useDimensions } from '@chakra-ui/react'; const Home = () => { const mainRef = useRef(); const dimensions = useDimensions(mainRef, true); const [size, setSize] = useState(''); useEffect(() => { if (dimensions) { let width = dimensions.borderBox.width; switch (true) { case width < 499: setSize('base'); break; case width >= 499 && width < 696: setSize('sm'); break; case width >= 696 && width < 945: setSize('md'); break; case width >= 945: setSize('lg'); break; } } }, [dimensions]); return ( <> <Nav /> <Box w="100vw" h="100vh" display="flex" justifyContent="center" ref={mainRef} alignItems="flex-start" as="main" > <Layout> <MyGrid /> <Box fontSize={{ base: '20px', sm: '24px' }} mt="30px"> <Text display="flex" justifyContent="center"> Width: {dimensions && dimensions.borderBox.width}px </Text>
 <Text display="flex" justifyContent="center"> Custom breakpoint size: {size} </Text>
 </Box>
 </Layout>
 </Box>
 </>
 );
};
export default Home;

On the additional load, the size state is a empty string const [size, setSize] = useState('') and will be updates in the useEffect, after the first render to the screen.

Every time the dimensions object from Chakra, then we'll use a switch statement to determine what to update the size state to.

You can see the source code here
And follow me here

Items.Title: PancakeSwap 和 Uniswap 之间的区别：概述 | Difference Between PancakeSwap and Uniswap: An Overview
Items.Description:
根据CoinMarketCap的数据，它在市场上运营的顶级 DEX 中排名第三。

像许多其他 DEX 一样，PancakeSwap 没有通过 IDO 或 ICO 筹集资金。 相反，交易所背后的团队自筹了所有资金。 该平台由财政部资助，财政部从所有交易费用中赚取 12% 的佣金。

流动性提供者与 PancakeSwap 的糖浆池交互并接收称为 $FLIP 的代币。 这些代币代表他们在池中的份额。 用户还可以购买另外两个名为 $CAKE 的代币，即他们的原生代币和 $SYRUP。 此外，由于该平台在 BSC 上运行，它支持数以千计的 BEP-20 代币。

什么是 Uniswap？

它是第一个达到与一些中心化同行一样的人气和交易量水平的 DEX。 作为首批自动做市商 (AMM) 之一，Uniswap 允许用户扮演流动性提供者 (LP) 的角色，并向分散的流动性池贡献资金。 因此，有限合伙人获得了矿池产生的一小部分费用，作为被动收入的来源。

自推出以来，Uniswap 的交易量已超过 8 亿美元，并举办了最赚钱的空投活动之一。

竞争

在 2020 年令人难以置信的 DeFi 夏天之后，Uniswap 确立了自己在市场上运营的 DEX 中的头号竞争者的地位。 用户将 Uniswap 视为 DEX 的基准。

尽管大多数以 DeFi 为重点的项目都愿意承担这一职责，但 PancakeSwap 却成为了一个强大的竞争者。 在 2021 年的前 49 天内，PancakeSwap 的交易量从微薄的 3700 万美元飙升至惊人的 1B 美元。

然而，这种令人难以置信的增长归因于托管 Uniswap 平台的以太坊区块链的缺点。 由于依赖工作量证明（PoW）共识机制，ETH 遇到了可扩展性问题。 因此，平台上的拥堵导致网络上的高昂gas费用。 股权证明 (PoS) 计划于今年 8 月进行。

用户可以通过提供流动性、质押和在交易所购买来积累更多的 $CAKE 代币。 此外，用户可以在公开的加密市场上交易发行给 LP 的 $CAKE 代币。

它使用户能够获得 40% 的 $CAKE 排放作为奖励。

  • 采用率 为该类别选择单个 DEX 具有挑战性。 与此同时，PancakeSwap 的采用率也在高效增长。

然而，尽管 Uniswap 在交易量方面领先，但 PancakeSwap 的活跃用户却比 Uniswap 多。

  • 交易成本 以太坊在这场比赛中输掉了比赛，为其他具有智能合约能力的平台提供了探索机会的空间。 随着 Uniswap 在 ETH 平台上运行，Gas 费用呈指数增长。

对于需要进行少量交易的 Uniswap 用户来说，这是一个相当大的问题。 否则，汽油费将占他们利润的一部分。 另一方面， PancakeSwap可以提供显着降低的交易成本，因为它使用交易更便宜的区块链（币安智能链）。 除了降低交易成本外，BSC 还具有更好的交易速度。

  • 交易量和流动性 Uniswap 仍然处于领先地位，尽管 PancakeSwap 上有相当大的流动性。 与 PancakeSwap 相比，Uniswap 上的大多数代币具有更高的流动性。

截至目前，在每日交易量方面，Uniswap 占据上风。 由于 DeFi 生态系统的波动性，PancakeSwap 必须保持上升轨迹以匹配 Uniswap 并在交易量方面击败后者。

下一步是什么？

这两个 DEX 之间的竞争已经超越了自身，因为它变成了以太坊和 BSC 区块链的优缺点之间的较量。
尽管Uniswap目前在交易量方面遥遥领先，但 PancakeSwap 拥有庞大的用户群，这得益于 BSC 区块链的成功。 如果以太坊 2.0 能够成功推出，Uniswap 可能会获得更大的吸引力。

日益激烈的竞争给以太坊开发人员带来了巨大压力，要求他们加快制定最终形式的路线图的最后期限，从而结束其当前的扩展问题。



原文:

PancakeSwap is a Decentralized Exchange (DEX) powered by the Binance Smart Chain (BSC), which operates through an Automated Market Maker (AMM) to complete the orders. PancakeSwap replaces an order book with a liquidity pool. According to CoinMarketCap, it ranks third among the top DEXs operating in the market.

Like many other DEXs, PancakeSwap did not raise money through IDO or ICO. Instead, the team working behind the exchange bootstrapped all the funds. The platform is funded through the Treasury, which earns 12% of the commission from all transaction fees.

Liquidity providers interact with the PancakeSwap’s Syrup Pools and receive tokens called $FLIP. These tokens represent their share in the pool. Users can also farm for two other tokens named $CAKE, their native token, and $SYRUP. Moreover, since the platform operates on BSC, it supports thousands of BEP-20 tokens.

What is Uniswap?

Uniswap is the undisputed king of DEXs. It was the first DEX to attain the level of popularity and trading volume like some of its centralized counterparts. Being one of the first Automated Market Makers (AMM), Uniswap allows the users to assume the role of liquidity providers (LPs) and contribute funds to decentralized liquidity pools. Consequently, the LPs gain a fraction of the fees generated by the pool as a source of passive income.

Launched in 2018, Uniswap has gone through multiple upgrades. Uniswap is running on its third version with improved capital efficiency, multiple fee tiers, and better oracles.

Since its launch, Uniswap has amassed more than $800M in trading volume and hosted one of the most profitable Airdrops.

The Rivalry

After an incredible DeFi summer in 2020, Uniswap established itself as the top contender among DEXs operating in the market. Users viewed Uniswap as a benchmark for DEXs.

While most DeFi-focused projects were willing to take up that mantle, PancakeSwap emerged as a formidable contender. Within the first 49 days of 2021, PancakeSwap’s volume boomed from a meager $37M to a staggering $1B.

However, this incredible growth was attributed to the shortcomings of the Ethereum blockchain, which hosts the Uniswap platform. ETH suffered scalability issues due to its reliance on the Proof-of-Work (PoW) consensus mechanism. Consequently, the congestion on the platform gave rise to high gas fees on the network. The Proof-of-Stake (PoS) shift is scheduled for August this year.

PancakeSwap vs. Uniswap: A Comparison

*$CAKE vs. $UNI
*
The base asset of PancakeSwap is its native token $CAKE, which works as its governance token while providing farming and staking functionalities. Users can accumulate more $CAKE tokens by providing liquidity, staking, and purchasing them on an exchange. Moreover, users can trade $CAKE tokens issued to LPs in the open crypto market.

The native token of Uniswap, known as $UNI, was airdropped to the platform users back in September 2020. The actual use case of the token is to facilitate decentralized governance.

PancakeSwap has another token called $SYRUP. It enables users to receive 40% of $CAKE emissions as rewards.

  • Adoption Rate It is challenging to select a single DEX for this category. Uniswap is much older and has a formidable base of users. At the same time, the adoption rate of PancakeSwap is growing efficiently.

Uniswap introduced the first AMM functionalities, which no other project at the time could successfully do so. However, PancakeSwap has more active users than Uniswap, even though Uniswap leads in terms of the trading volume.

  • Transaction Costs Ethereum lost the contest on this one, making room for other platforms with smart contract capability to explore the opportunities. Gas fees have been rising exponentially with Uniswap running on the ETH platform.

This is quite a problem for Uniswap users who need to transact small amounts. Otherwise, the gas fees would take a part of their profits. PancakeSwap, on the other hand, can offer dramatically lower transaction costs as it uses a blockchain with cheaper transactions (Binance Smart Chain). Apart from lowering transaction costs, BSC also has better transaction speed.

  • Trading Volume and Liquidity Uniswap still enjoys the leading position, although there is considerable liquidity on PancakeSwap. Most of the tokens on Uniswap have significantly more liquidity when compared to PancakeSwap.

As of now, Uniswap has the upper hand when it comes to daily trading volume. Due to the volatility in the DeFi ecosystem, PancakeSwap has to maintain an upward trajectory to match Uniswap and beat the latter in terms of the trading volume.

What’s next?

The rivalry between these two DEXs extends beyond themselves as it becomes a tussle between the advantages and shortcomings of the Ethereum and the BSC blockchains.
The high trading volumes on BSC indicate the demand for cheaper and faster transactions. Although Uniswap is quite ahead in terms of trading volume right now, PancakeSwap has a formidable user base thriving on the success of the BSC blockchain. If Ethereum 2.0 can successfully roll out, Uniswap might gain a lot more traction.

The increased competition has put immense pressure on Ethereum developers to speed up the deadlines marking the roadmap leading to its proof-of-stake final form, ending its current scaling issues.

Items.Title: 基于 AI 的混合现实在手术中的 5 个真实世界应用 | 5 real-world applications of AI-based mixed reality in surgeries
Items.Description:
混合现实 (MR) 通过加速慢性病诊断过程，使护理时间更有效，实现个性化和改进的患者护理体验，正在彻底改变医疗保健行业。

在医疗保健领域的手术部分，需要大量的计划、准备和精确度。 医生在为患者进行手术时不能失去注意力。 手术中的混合现实旨在增强医生的能力，并为他们提供必要的手术工具和信息，而不会浪费任何时间。 因此，混合现实通过使外科医生能够与其他外科医生远程协作治疗可能在数千英里之外的患者，从而改变了手术过程。

通过医疗保健中的混合现实应用，外科医生可以虚拟执行各种手术，护士可以提高他们的技能。 总而言之，混合现实使利用技术为关键手术的医生进行无缝的术前计划和手术视觉辅助并将风险降至最低成为可能。

原文:

Mixed reality (MR) is revolutionizing the healthcare industry by accelerating the chronic illness diagnoses process, making the care time more effective, enabling a personalized and improved patient care experience.

In the surgery part of the healthcare domain, there’s a need for a lot of planning, preparation and precision. A doctor cannot afford to lose focus while performing surgery on a patient. Mixed reality in surgeries aims to empower doctors and provide them with the necessary surgical tools and information without any waste of time. Therefore, mixed reality has transformed the surgery procedure by enabling surgeons to remotely collaborate with other surgeons on patients who could be thousands of miles away.

With mixed reality applications in healthcare, surgeons can perform various operations virtually and nurses can polish their skillset. All in all, mixed reality makes it feasible to leverage technologies to make seamless pre-procedural planning and operative visual assistance to doctors for critical surgeries and minimize risks.

Items.Title: .NET Core 中的 gRPC 介绍 - iFour Technolab | Introduction to gRPC in .NET Core - iFour Technolab
Items.Description:
一元 RPC，客户端向服务器发送请求并接收相同的响应。

  • 客户端使用重新提供的流写入一系列消息并将它们发送到服务器的客户端流式 RPC。
  • 双向流式 RPC，其中客户端和服务器双向交换消息。
  • 无论客户端向服务器发送参与消息的调用并接收一系列回复，服务器流式 RPC。

    • 如果我们将 gRPC 与 Web API 进行比较，区别在于，

    • 创建 gRPC 服务

      我们将要构建的应用程序是一个微服务，它可以根据客户的类型（黄金或白金或白银）计算折扣，这可以是高级的。

      让我们首先创建一个新文件夹 gRPC-dotnet-microservice，添加客户端和服务应用程序。

      导航到此文件夹并通过在控制台窗口中键入以下命令来创建服务器项目。

      上面的命令创建了一个新模板。 定义合同

      第一步是定义合约，它是一个接口，告诉您服务打开的功能或功能。 在 gRPC 框架中，这个接口是由协议缓冲区或 protobuf 定义的。 该接口在 .proto 文件中专门定义。

       syntax = "proto3"; option csharp_namespace = "DiscountService"; package Discount; service DiscountAmount { rpc Amount (CalculateRequest) returns (CalculateReply); } message CalculateRequest { string customertype = 1; } message CalculateReply { double customerdiscount = 1; }

      让我们逐行理解。 前两行使用正在使用的 proto buff 版本和 C# 命名空间的语法。 在这种情况下，下一行告诉包名称 = 折扣。

      定义合同后，您需要创建可识别新 proto 文件的应用程序。 更新下面的DiscountService.csproj 文件，

       <project sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web"> <propertygroup> <targetframework>netcoreapp3.1</targetframework> </propertygroup> <itemgroup> <protobuf grpcservices="Server" include="Protosdiscount-service.proto"></protobuf></itemgroup> <itemgroup> <packagereference include="Grpc.AspNetCore" version="2.27.0"> </packagereference></itemgroup> </project>

      阅读更多：如何保护 Asp.net Core 中的公共 API？

      服务实施

      导航到 services 文件夹，将 GreeterService.cs 文件重命名为 DiscountService.cs，并将内容更改为以下内容。

       using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Grpc.Core; using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging; namespace DiscountService { public class DiscountAmountService :DiscountAmount.DiscountAmountBase { private readonlyILogger<discountamountservice> _logger; public DiscountAmountService(ILogger<discountamountservice> logger) { _logger = logger; } public override Task<calreply>AmountCalculate(CalRequest request, ServerCallContext context) { return Task.FromResult(new CalReply { Customerdiscount=ReturnDiscount(request.Customertype) }); } private double ReturnDiscount(string customertype) { double discount=0.0; if (customertype=="GOLD") { discount=15.6; } else if (customertype=="PLATINUM") { discount=20.6; } else if (customertype=="DIAMOND") { discount=25.6; } return discount; } } } </calreply></discountamountservice></discountamountservice>

      我们正在实现可从 DiscountAmount.DiscountAmountBase 类继承的 DiscountAmountService 类。 这个基类是在构建时从 .proto 文件中包含的数据生成的。

      它具有 AmountCalculate 函数，该函数实现 .proto 文件中的 rpc 定义，并将 CalculatorReply 和 CalculateRequest 类型作为返回类型，将请求参数定义为消息类型。

      创建 gRPC 客户端

      导航到文件夹 `grpc-dotnet-microservice 并通过在 cmd 中键入以下命令来创建一个新项目。

      上述命令将在创建 DiscountCalClient 的文件夹中创建一个控制台应用程序。 现在移动到此文件夹并通过键入以下命令添加所需的依赖项。

      现在客户端应用程序需要了解有关如何请求服务器应用程序、要传递哪些参数以及可能的返回类型的详细信息。

      正在寻找专用的ASP.NET Core Web 开发人员？ 您的搜索到此结束。

      这是通过添加预定义的 proto 文件来完成的。 第一个是在 DiscountCalClient 中创建一个 proto 文件夹，并从 gRPC 服务中复制 discount-service.proto 并通过添加 .proto 文件的引用来更新 DiscountCalClient.csproj 项目文件。

      ` `

      Exe netcoreapp3.1 exe netcoreapp3.1



    原文:

    gRPC is an open-source remote process call system developed at Google in 2015.The supporting programming languages like C++, Java (+ Android), Objective-C (iOS), Python, Ruby, Go, C#, NodeJS and others.It uses HTTP / 2 to transfer binary messages and through a default protocol buffer as an interface definition language (IDL) to describe the service interface and the composition of the messages.

    
service Greeter
{  
  rpcSayHi (HiRequest) returns (HiReply) {}  
}  

message HiRequest {  
    string name = 1;  
}  

message HiReply {  
    string message = 1;  
}

    The gRPC defines two types of service methods,

    • Unary RPCs, where the client sends a request to the server and receives the same response.
    • Client streaming RPCs wherever the client writes a sequence of messages and sends them to the server, using the re-provided stream.
    • Bidirectional streaming RPC where the client and the server exchange messages in both directions.
    • Server Streaming RPC wherever the client sends a call for participation message to the server and receives a sequence of replies.

    If we compare gRPC to Web API, differences are,

    • The Web API is based on the REST architecture where the gRPC proposes RPC model, a model where a remote process is demanded on the server as a client.
    • The Web API uses HTTP for transport while the gRPC uses HTTP / 2.
    • The data exchanged by the Web API is a human readable format (especially JSON), while the gRPC uses a compact binary format.

    Creating a gRPC service

    The application we will be going to build is a microservice that calculates a discount based on the type of customer -- Gold or Platinum or Silver -- Which can be advanced.

    Let’s start by creating a new folder, gRPC-dotnet-microservice, adding both client and service applications.

    Navigate to this folder and create a server project by typing the following command in the console window.

    The above command creates a new template. .NET Core gRPC project in the DiscountServices folder.

    Defining the Contract

    The first step is to define the contract which is an interface that tells you the functionality or functions open by the service. In the gRPC framework, this interface is defined by the protocol buffer or protobuf. This interface is specifically defined in the .proto file.

    
syntax = "proto3";  

  option csharp_namespace = "DiscountService";  
  package Discount;  

service DiscountAmount 
{  
  rpc Amount (CalculateRequest) returns (CalculateReply);  
}  

message CalculateRequest 
{  
  string customertype = 1;  
}  

message CalculateReply 
{  
  double customerdiscount = 1;  
}

    Let’s understand line by line. The first two lines use the syntax of the proto buff version in use and C# namespace. The next line tells the package name in this case = Discount.

    Once the contract is defined, you need to create the application aware of the new proto file. Update theDiscountService.csproj file below,

            <project sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
<propertygroup>
<targetframework>netcoreapp3.1</targetframework>
</propertygroup>
<itemgroup>
<protobuf grpcservices="Server" include="Protosdiscount-service.proto"></protobuf></itemgroup>
<itemgroup>
<packagereference include="Grpc.AspNetCore" version="2.27.0">
</packagereference></itemgroup>
</project>

    Read More: How To Secure Public Apis In Asp.net Core?

    Service implementation

    Navigate to the services folder, rename the GreeterService.cs file to DiscountService.cs, and change the contents with below.

    
using System;  
using System.Collections.Generic;  
using System.Linq;  
using System.Threading.Tasks;  
using Grpc.Core;  
using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;  
namespace DiscountService
{  
    public class DiscountAmountService :DiscountAmount.DiscountAmountBase
    {  
        private readonlyILogger<discountamountservice> _logger;  
        public DiscountAmountService(ILogger<discountamountservice> logger)  
        {  
            _logger = logger;  
        }  
        public override Task<calreply>AmountCalculate(CalRequest request, ServerCallContext context)  
        {  
            return Task.FromResult(new CalReply
            {  
        Customerdiscount=ReturnDiscount(request.Customertype)  
            });  
        }   
        private double ReturnDiscount(string customertype)  
        {  
            double discount=0.0;  
            if (customertype=="GOLD")  
            {  
                discount=15.6;   
            }  
            else if (customertype=="PLATINUM")  
            {  
                discount=20.6;  
            }  
            else if (customertype=="DIAMOND")  
            {  
                discount=25.6;  
            }  
            return discount;  
        }  
    }  
} </calreply></discountamountservice></discountamountservice>

    We're implementing to the DiscountAmountService class that's heritable from the DiscountAmount.DiscountAmountBase class. This base class is generated from the data contained in the .proto file at build time.

    It has AmountCalculate function which implements the rpc definition in the .proto file and the CalculatorReply and CalculateRequest types as return types and request parameters defined as a message types.

    Creating a gRPC client

    Navigate to the folder ` grpc-dotnet-microservice and make a new project by typing the following command in cmd.

    The above command will create a console application in the folder created DiscountCalClient. Now move to this folder and add the required dependencies by typing the following commands.

    `

    dotnet add DiscountCalClient.csproj package Grpc.Net.Client dotnet add DiscountCalClient.csproj package Google.Protobuf

    dotnet add DiscountCalClient.csproj package Grpc.Tools

    `

    Now the client application needs to be aware of the details about how to Request server application, which parameters to pass, and what will be the possible return type.

    Searching for Dedicated ASP.NET Core Web Developer? Your Search ends here.

    This is done by adding a predefined proto file. First one is to create a proto folder inside DiscountCalClient and copy the discount-service.proto from the gRPC service and update the DiscountCalClient.csproj Project file by adding a reference of .proto file.

    `

    Exe netcoreapp3.1

Items.Title: 用构建块解释的学习方法 | Learning Methodologies Explained with Building Blocks
Items.Description:
学习方法可能是复杂而乏味的。 但是，如果您向客户解释它们，则可以避免很多复杂性。

如果您打开这篇文章是为了说服您的客户，让他们在喝咖啡的同时对前 3 种流行的 eLearning 开发方法（ADDIE、AGILE 和 SAM）进行无行话的概述，那么您就得到了您想要的。

拉里、卷毛、萌和……积木？

任何培训发展都归结为遵循一组标准的任务，这些任务定义了学习成果的效率和质量。 我们将这些标准称为学习方法。多年来，这些标准已演变为三种主要策略：

我们不会直接讨论这些方法之间的差异，而是讨论希望为他们的学校项目建造乐高建筑的拉里、卷毛和莫。

第一栋楼：ADDIE
拉里选择 ADDIE 方法来建造他的摩天大楼。

分析：他从老师那里得到关于楼层、窗户和门的数量的输入。

设计：他创建平面图并得到老师的批准。

发展：他从商店购买乐高积木，跳过他最喜欢的卡通节目，并在睡前建造这座建筑。

实施：他仔细地包装他的项目并将其带到学校提交。

评价：他的老师根据批准的设计检查乐高建筑，并给拉里 A+。 一切都很好。

但是如果拉里的老师建议一楼应该有一个额外的窗户和一个更宽的门呢？ 拉里将不得不再次跳过卡通节目并重做完整的建筑。 相当乏味，对吧？

更聪明的方法：敏捷
他选择了敏捷方法：

然后，他将原型展示给他的老师，老师建议进行一些更改。 他重复步骤 2 和 3，直到他的老师签字为止。 然后，Curly 高兴地提交了建筑物进行最终评估。 成功！

在现实生活中，我们使用 Sprints、Scrums 和 Product Backlogs 等更花哨和更复杂的术语来描述这些阶段，但核心思想是相同的。

但是，如果每次 Curly 展示他的进步，老师都会建议一些新的变化呢？ 这将创建一个无休止的审查和返工循环。

更好的方法：SAM
这让他的老师很开心，也节省了他的午餐时间。

他建造了三分之一的建筑并展示给他的老师。 他还就下一阶段的发展提供了进一步的意见。 萌根据老师的建议重做，下一部分工作，提交建筑审核； 现在，三分之二的工作已经完成。 他再次重复最后一步，然后最终提交他的项目进行审查。

在现实生活中，根据项目规模和复杂性，审查阶段的数量可能会扩展到 8 次迭代。 但主要思想仍然几乎相同。

哪种方法更适合电子学习发展？
尽管在很大程度上取决于场景，但 Moe 采用了更聪明的方法：

限制迭代审查的数量 在每个阶段纳入反馈和建议 建筑物的最终版本几乎是老师所期望的，相对较少的审查。

现在，您知道如何向您的客户解释 ADDIE、AGILE 和 SAM，您的咖啡就快喝完了。

如果您想考虑一下，请重新填充。 我们利用尖端技术和快速创作工具来提供交互式学习内容。 最近，Hornbillfx 被评为印度电子学习开发公司前三名。

帕特里夏乔安妮 - 迪特马尔·戴姆林 - May 26 5月26日



原文:

Learning methodologies can be complex and boring. But in case you are explaining them to a client, much of the complexity can be avoided.

If you opened this article hoping to convince your client by giving them a jargon-less overview of the top 3 popular eLearning development methodologies — ADDIE, AGILE, and SAM over a coffee, you got just what you wanted.

Larry, Curly, Moe, and … Building Blocks?

Any training development comes down to following a standard set of tasks that define the efficiency and quality of the learning outcome. We call these standards learning methodologies.Over the years, these standards have evolved to form three main strategies:

ADDIE (Analyze, Design, Develop Implement, Evaluate) AGILE SAM (Successive Approximation Model)

Instead of directly talking about the differences between these methodologies, we will talk about Larry, Curly and Moe who wish to construct a Lego building for their school project.

The first building: ADDIE
Larry chooses ADDIE approach to build his skyscraper.

Analysis: He gets the inputs from his teacher about the number of storeys, windows, and doors.

Design: He creates the floor plan and gets it approved by his teacher.

Development: He buys Lego blocks from the store, skips his favorite cartoon show and constructs the building before his bedtime.

Implementation: He carefully wraps his project and takes it to school for submission.

Evaluation: His teacher checks the Lego building against the approved design, and gives Larry an A+. All is good.

But what if Larry’s teacher suggests the ground floor should have an additional window and a wider door? Larry will have to skip the cartoon show again and redo the complete construction. Pretty tedious, right?

A smarter approach: AGILE
Curly anticipates that his teacher may suggest some changes. He chooses the AGILE approach:

Curly makes a list of all the key features that his teacher mentions and builds a prototype. He then shows the prototype to his teacher, who suggests some changes. Curly incorporates those changes and submits it again for review. He repeats steps 2 & 3 till his teacher signs off. Curly then happily submits the building for the final evaluation. Success!

In real life, we use fancier and more complex jargon such as Sprints, Scrums and Product Backlogs to describe these stages, but the core idea is the same.

But, what if the teacher suggests some new changes every time Curly shows his progress? This will create an unending loop of review and rework.

An even better approach: SAM
Moe decides to use Curly’s approach but limits it to three reviews and three reworks. This makes his teacher happy as well as saves his lunchtime.

Moe collects all the necessary inputs. He builds one-third of the building and shows it to his teacher. Moe’s teacher suggests some changes in the design. He also gives further input on the next phase of development. Moe reworks based on his teacher’s suggestions, works on the next part, and submits the building for review; now, two-thirds of the work is complete. He repeats the last step once more and then finally submits his project for examination.

In real life, the number of review stages may scale up to 8 iterations depending on the project size and complexity. But the main idea remains pretty much the same.

Which approach is better for eLearning Development?
Although it largely depends on the scenarios, Moe adopted a smarter approach:

Limiting the number of iterative reviews Incorporating feedback and suggestions at every stage The final version of the building is pretty much what the teacher expected, comparatively with lesser reviews.

Now, you know how to explain ADDIE, AGILE, and SAM to your client and your coffee is almost finished.

Get a refill if you want to think about it. Hornbillfx is one of the leading custom eLearning solutions providers, we focus on corporate eLearning solutions, and custom eLearning design and deliver it in a cloud-based learning management system. We utilize cutting-edge technology and rapid authoring tools to deliver interactive learning content. Recently Hornbillfx ranked as top three Elearning development companies in India.

Patricia Joanne - May 21 '20

Dietmar Deimling - May 26

Items.Title: 我的 AWS SAA 认证之旅开始 | My Journey to AWS SAA Certification Begins
Items.Description:
我不知道我是从什么时候开始对 AWS 服务产生兴趣的，但我现在是 SheBuilds-CloudUp 计划的参与者，正在为我的第一个软件认证做准备。 我觉得我回到了学校——削尖铅笔，准备好笔记本，为第一天整理衣服——开个玩笑。 都是遥远的。 不过，这还是很令人兴奋的。 我知道; 我真的应该多出去走走。

无论如何，如果你不知道这个项目，它是一个灵活的、基于社区的女性学习项目。 查看这个SheBuilds-CloudUp SAA 计划页面了解更多详细信息。 哦，他们还有一个云从业者计划，可以在这个SheBuilds-CloudUp 云从业者计划中找到。

但是，您可以通过 AWS 的大量在线课程目录进行准备和学习，查看其白皮书和其他资源，以帮助您为 Cloud Practitioner 认证做好准备。 真的，有这么多资源可以帮助你，试图筛选所有信息真的会让人不知所措！ 一开始我肯定不知所措，因为我不知道从哪里开始。 无论如何，我稍后会在另一篇文章中提到这些资源。

凭借 AWS 解决方案架构师助理证书，您无需先获得云从业者证书即可参加 AWS SAA 考试。 这不是参加考试的先决条件，但建议至少完成 Cloud Practitioner 课程，或具备 AWS 服务经验。

好吧，那我是怎么到这里的？ 好吧，我在之前的项目中使用过 AWS S3 存储桶，当然这需要阅读 AWS S3 文档。 现在，我确信 AWS 的人员在他们的文档上努力工作，但浏览起来有点困难，而且查找有关 S3 存储桶配置和所有其他相关信息的信息并不是那么简单。

无论如何，我想找出我们可以利用 AWS 服务的其他方式，所以当 Teneika Askew 在 Twitter 上发布关于 SheBuilds-CloudUp 计划的推文时，我注册了。 你可以在 Twitter @teneikaask_you上找到她。 谢谢你，特内卡！

因此，云从业者考试的费用目前为 100 美元，AWS SAA 考试的费用为 150 美元。 您可以在AWS 证书定价页面找到当前定价。

这是奖励——SheBuilds-CloudUP 计划为您提供赞助的考试券，让您可以免费参加考试！ 这有多棒？

顺便说一句，如果你是一个想要过渡到科技行业的女性，这个项目将是一个获得高要求技能免费培训的好机会。 只需看看Glassdoor.com上亚马逊网络服务工作的工资数据。

感兴趣的？ 好吧，这里又是一个让你开始的链接： SheBuilds-CloudUp Cloud Practitioner Program 。 他们的计划每八周开始一次，因此如果该计划在两次会议之间，请确保将自己添加到候补名单中。

有了这个伟大的计划，我鼓励你加入我们！ 你可以这样做！



原文:

I don't know when I started to become intrigued with AWS services, but here I am, a participant of the SheBuilds-CloudUp Program, preparing for my first software certification. I feel like I'm back in school--getting my pencils sharpened, getting my notebooks ready, and laying out my clothes for the first day--just kidding about that last part. It's all remote. Still, it's pretty exciting. I know; I really should get out more.

Anyway, If you're not aware of this program, it's a flexible, community-based, learning program for women. Check out this SheBuilds-CloudUp SAA Program page for more details. Oh, they also have a cloud practitioner program which could be found at this SheBuilds-CloudUp Cloud Practitioner Program.

The AWS certification starts off with the Cloud Practitioner certification which "recommends" that you have six months of foundational knowledge or experience in AWS Cloud services--more info here on the AWS Certification page. However, you can prepare and learn through AWS's vast catalog of online classes, review their white papers, and other resources to help prepare you for the Cloud Practitioner certification. Really, there are so many resources out there to help you, it can really be overwhelming trying to sift through all the information! I was definitely overwhelmed at first because I wasn't sure where to start. In any case, I'll mention those resources later in another post.

With the AWS Solution Architect Associate certificate, you don't need to have the Cloud Practitioner Certificate first in order to take the AWS SAA exam. It's not a prerequisite to take the test, but it's recommended to at least go through the Cloud Practitioner lessons, or have experience with AWS services.

Okay, so how did I get here? Well, I had used AWS S3 buckets in a previous project, and of course that required reading the AWS S3 documentation. Now, I'm sure that the people at AWS work hard on their documentation, but it was a bit difficult to navigate through, and finding information about S3 bucket configurations and all the other things that go with it, wasn't that straightforward.

In any case, I wanted to find out other ways we could leverage AWS services, so when Teneika Askew tweeted about the SheBuilds-CloudUp program on Twitter, I signed up. You can find her on Twitter @teneikaask_you. Thank you, Teneika!

So, the cost for the Cloud Practitioner Exam is currently $100 and the AWS SAA Exam is $150. You can find the current pricing at AWS Certificate Pricing page.

Here's the bonus--the SheBuilds-CloudUP program gives you a sponsored exam voucher which will allow you to take the exam for free! How awesome is that?

By the way, if you're a woman who wants to transition into tech, this program would be a great opportunity to get free training for a high demand skill. Just take a look at the salary data here for Amazon Web Services jobs at Glassdoor.com.

Interested? Well, here's the link again to start you off: SheBuilds-CloudUp Cloud Practitioner Program. Their program starts every eight weeks, so make sure you add yourself to the waitlist if the program is in between sessions.

With this great program, I encourage you to join us! You CAN do this!

Items.Title: 社交媒体营销自动化：如何像专业人士一样实施 | Social Media Marketing Automation: How to Implement it Like a Pro
Items.Description:
当您花费大部分时间手动发布到社交媒体或为您的团队创建报告时，可能很难创建社交媒体营销的最关键方面之一，即客户关系。 利用社交媒体自动化提供的空闲时间，您将能够专注于通过个性化消息、帖子、评论等与客户建立实际关系。

如果您活跃于多个社交媒体网络，社交媒体营销自动化将非常有用。 您可能在每个网络上共享一些不同的内容，因此很难跟踪您发布到每个网络的时间和内容。 当您自动化您的社交媒体策略时，您可以跨多个平台安排帖子，从而更轻松地掌握特定于平台的主题和议程。

如果你不定期写作，用户会认为你不太可靠，一个月发几篇文章也不可能让你处于边缘地位。 您可以通过社交媒体自动化提前安排帖子，以确保所有渠道的一致性。

社交媒体自动化的有效性可以为搜索引擎营销活动提供许多优势，没有它就很难实现。

阅读更多：-社交美的营销自动化



原文:

It can be difficult to create one of the most crucial aspects of social media marketing i.e. customer relationships when you spend the majority of your time manually publishing to social media or creating reports for your team. You’ll be able to focus on creating actual relationships with clients via personalized messaging, posts, comments, and more with the spare time provided by social media automation.

If you’re active on more than one social media network, social media marketing automation is very useful. You’re probably sharing a bit of distinct content on each network, making it difficult to keep track of when and what you’re posting to each. When you automate your social media strategy, you can schedule posts across many platforms, making it easier to stay on top of platform-specific topics and agendas.

If you don’t write on a regular basis, users will perceive you as less reliable, and a few postings a month won’t likely remain you on the edge. You may schedule posts in advance with social media automation to ensure consistency across all channels.

The effectiveness of social media automation can provide a number of advantages to a search engine marketing campaign that is difficult to achieve without it.

Read more:- Social Midea Marketing Automation

Items.Title: 在 Kubernetes 中使用私有 docker 注册表 | Using private docker registry inside kubernetes
Items.Description:
使用 Digital Ocean Container Registry 配置客户 k8s 集群。

要求

  • 私有 docker 注册表（我使用 Digital Ocean 进行演示）
  • 访问像Dockerhub这样的公共 Docker 注册表中的公共镜像是 Kubernetes 集群的默认行为，除非您出于明显的安全原因想要集成自己的私有注册表来访问私有 Docker 镜像。 但是配置一个裸机 kubernetes集群以访问您的私有注册表需要几个步骤，包括更新部署/pod yaml。 我正在使用数字海洋码头注册作为参考。 但是大多数提供商的步骤是相同的​​。

如果您没有用于演示的集群。 签出种类。 或者你可以使用 Katacoda 或Play with kubernetes

第1步

首先，我们必须将集群配置为向私有注册表进行身份验证。 我们可以通过两种方法做到这一点。 第一种方法是使用config.json文件，第二种方法需要您的用户名、密码和电子邮件。
在数字海洋容器注册表中，您可以在容器注册表主页操作按钮处下载只读配置文件。 当您在本地机器上登录到 docker 注册表时，可以在~/.docker/config.json中找到相同的文件。

使用config.json

 kubectl create secret generic registry-credential \ --from-file = .dockerconfigjson = <path/to/.docker/config.json> \ --type = kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

或者

使用 docker 凭据

kubectl create secret docker-registry registry-credential --docker-server = <your-registry-server> --docker-username = <your-name> --docker-password = <your-pword> --docker-email = <your-email>

检查创建的秘密

kubectl get secret registry-credential --output = yaml

输出类似于：

 apiVersion : v1 kind : Secret metadata : ... name : registry-credential ... data : .dockerconfigjson : eyJodHRwczovL2luZGV4... type : kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

要将数据转换为可读格式，请使用以下命令。

 kubectl get secret registry-credential --output = "jsonpath={.data. \. dockerconfigjson}" | base64 --decode

第2步

编辑吊舱

创建秘密后，我们可以更新我们的 pod 配置以使用创建的秘密来提取图像。 如下更新您的 pod 的规范会话。

 spec : containers : - name : private-reg-container image : <your-private-image> imagePullSecrets : - name : registry-credential

要从私有注册表中提取映像，Kubernetes 需要凭据。 配置文件中的imagePullSecrets字段指定 Kubernetes 应该从名为registry-credential的 Secret 获取凭证。 创建一个使用您的 Secret 的 Pod，并验证该 Pod 是否正在运行

kubectl apply -f pod.yaml kubectl get pod <pod-name>

如果您不想将imagePullSecrets添加到您的 pod 配置中，或者需要将每个命名空间的不同注册表设置为默认值，请转到第 3 步。

第 3 步

将图像拉取机密添加到服务帐户

如果您想预览有关服务帐户的信息，请查看此文档
要将我们的新密钥添加到命名空间，我们必须修改服务帐户并将我们的新密钥名称推送到服务帐户内的imagePullSecret数组。 我们可以通过以下方式下载服务帐户清单

kubectl get serviceaccounts default -o yaml > ./service-account.yaml

然后更新如下：

 apiVersion : v1 kind : ServiceAccount metadata : creationTimestamp : 2015-08-07T22:02:39Z name : default namespace : default uid : 052fb0f4-3d50-11e5-b066-42010af0d7b6 secrets : - name : default-token-uudge imagePullSecrets : - name : registry-credential

最后用更新的清单文件替换服务帐户

kubectl replace serviceaccount default -f ./service-account.yaml

现在，当在当前命名空间中创建新 Pod 并使用默认 ServiceAccount 时，新 Pod 的spec.imagePullSecrets字段会自动设置。

参考：
将图像拉取密码添加到服务帐户

拉吉特保罗_#SaveSoil - Apr 17 4月17日



原文:

Configure a customer k8s cluster with Digital Ocean Container Registry.

Requirements

  • Kubernetes cluster (Access to the cluster via kubectl)
  • Private docker registry (I use Digital Ocean for demo)
  • Docker image

Accessing public images in a public docker registry like Dockerhub is the default behaviour of a kubernetes cluster, unless you want to integrate your own private registry to access private docker images for obvious security reasons. Cloud service providers like AWS, Digital Ocean provides easy integration between own docker registry to managed kubernetes.

But configuring a baremetal kubernetes cluster to access your private registry comes with couple of steps including updating the deployment/pod yaml. I am using digital ocean docker registry for the reference. But steps are same for most of the providers.

If you don't have a cluster for demo. Check out Kind. Kind helps you to run a kubernetes cluster locally in minutes. Or else you can use Katacoda or Play with kubernetes

Step 1

Dockerconfig.json

First we have to get the cluster configured to authenticate to the private registry. A Kubernetes cluster uses the Secret of kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson type to authenticate with a container registry to pull a private image. We can do it in two methods. First method is to use the config.json file and the second method requires your username, password and email.
In digital ocean container registry you can download a read-only config file at container registry home Actions button. The same file can be found in ~/.docker/config.json when you login in to docker registry in your local machine.

Using config.json 

kubectl create secret generic registry-credential \ --from-file=.dockerconfigjson=<path/to/.docker/config.json> \ --type=kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

or

Using docker credentials 

kubectl create secret docker-registry registry-credential --docker-server=<your-registry-server> --docker-username=<your-name> --docker-password=<your-pword> --docker-email=<your-email>

Inspect created secret

kubectl get secret registry-credential --output=yaml

The output is similar to this:

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata: ... name: registry-credential ...
data: .dockerconfigjson: eyJodHRwczovL2luZGV4...
type: kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

The value of the .dockerconfigjson field is a base64 representation of your Docker credentials. To convert the data into readable format use the following command.

kubectl get secret registry-credential --output="jsonpath={.data.\.dockerconfigjson}" | base64 --decode

Step 2

Editing the pod

Once the secret is created we can update our pod config to use the created secret to pull images. Update the spec session of your pod as follows.

spec: containers: - name: private-reg-container image: <your-private-image> imagePullSecrets: - name: registry-credential

To pull the image from the private registry, Kubernetes needs credentials. The imagePullSecrets field in the configuration file specifies that Kubernetes should get the credentials from a Secret named registry-credential. Create a Pod that uses your Secret, and verify that the Pod is running

kubectl apply -f pod.yaml
kubectl get pod <pod-name>

If you don't want to add imagePullSecrets to your pod config or need to set different registries per namespace as default then go to step 3.

Step 3

Adding image pull secretes to service account

If you want to get a preview about service accounts checkout this doc
To add our new secret to the namespace we have to modify the service account and push our new secret name to the imagePullSecret array inside the service account. We can download the service account manifest by

kubectl get serviceaccounts default -o yaml > ./service-account.yaml

then update it as follows:

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: creationTimestamp: 2015-08-07T22:02:39Z name: default namespace: default uid: 052fb0f4-3d50-11e5-b066-42010af0d7b6
secrets:
- name: default-token-uudge
imagePullSecrets:
- name: registry-credential

Finally replace the service account with updated manifest file

kubectl replace serviceaccount default -f ./service-account.yaml

Now, when a new Pod is created in the current namespace and using the default ServiceAccount, the new Pod has its spec.imagePullSecrets field set automatically.

References:
Pull an Image from a Private Registry
Add image pull secret to service account
Add image pull secret to service account

Rajit Paul_#SaveSoil - Apr 17

Items.Title: 减少 WordPress 网站加载时间的 10 种行之有效的方法 | 10 Proven Ways to Reduce WordPress Website Load Time
Items.Description:
普里亚莱

发表于您想提高或提高 WordPress 的速度吗？ 特别是 WordPress，目前是启动新网站的最佳和最受欢迎的平台之一。 另一方面，每日金额继续上升。 但是，有一个问题：大量用户在提供反馈，而 WordPress 速度很慢。 因此，他们有兴趣学习如何让 WordPress 运行得更快。 您不知道 WordPress 并没有您想象的那么慢。 它比市场上任何其他 CMS 都要快得多。 但是，您必须确保以正确的方式加速 WordPress。

提高 WordPress 速度的基础知识

我将介绍一些基本的问题和答案，以及加速 WordPress 的方法。 毫无疑问，它将帮助您扩展知识库并学习提高 WordPress 速度的基础知识。 让我们谈谈如何正确加速WordPress。

拥有一个快速加载的 WordPress 网站有什么好处？

您是否知道访问者加载网站仅需 7 秒，在某些情况下甚至更短？ 根据亚马逊的说法，如果他们的网站加载时间超过一秒，他们就会失去 7% 的转化率。 它甚至导致页面浏览量下降 11%，客户满意度下降 16%。
你能想象网站所有者会损失多少钱吗？ 另一方面，谷歌认为速度较慢的网站是值得的。 同样，尽量远离网站的范围。 牢记这一点，加速您的 WordPress 网站至关重要。

如何检查您的 WordPress 网站的速度？

那么，您能告诉我如何测试我的 WordPress 网站的速度吗？ 是的！ 如果您认为网站在几秒钟内即可加载，为什么还要仔细检查它们？ 另一方面，来自不同位置或计算机的新访问者将无法这样做。 因此，您可以使用 Pingdom 来测试 WordPress 网站的加载速度。 首先要记住的是，它是一个完全免费且非常有用的工具，用于确定 WordPress 网站的速度。
请记住，Pingdom 是一项免费服务，可让您在不同位置测试您的网站。 值得注意的是，在两秒内加载的网站是理想的，如果您可以稍微改进一下，您将获得更忠诚的客户。 尤其是毫秒，它会对您的 WordPress 网站的用户体验产生重大影响，而您甚至可能没有意识到这一点。

是什么减慢了 WordPress 网站的速度？

由于各种原因，WordPress 网站可能会变慢。 当您查看网站时，您将获得统计数据和网站速度变慢的原因。 也会有一些技术上的考虑。 但是，下面列出的原因是 WordPress 网站速度变慢的最常见原因。 网站托管 - 如果您的托管设置不正确，可能会导致您的网站加载缓慢。 您还必须安装缓存插件和其他扩展。 继续阅读; 我稍后会解释。 未优化的页面 - 包含大量大图像的页面可能会降低您的网站速度。 不必要的插件——插件会导致 WordPress 变慢。

外部脚本，例如广告和字体加载器，可能会导致 WordPress 网站速度变慢。

快速提高WordPress的速度：

因此，您已经意识到提高 WordPress 速度的重要性。 我将在本文中介绍如何使 WordPress 更快。 采纳建议并在您的网站上付诸实践。 它将显着提高 WordPress 的速度并帮助您在 Google 上排名，并改变您网站的用户体验。

如果您使用两种方法（安装缓存插件和优化图像），您的网站将非常快速地加载并在多个位置进行测试。 同样，完成也不会花很长时间。 现在由您决定是否让您的 WordPress 网站保持最新状态。 因此，让 WordPress 网站保持最新状态至关重要。 它将帮助您加速 WordPress、提高安全性并总体上优化您的网站。 同样，定期更新所有插件将确保 WordPress 运行得更快。

这是一种简单的方法，不需要对您网站的代码进行任何更改，并且非常有效。 实际上，您的网站页面是动态的。 因此，它的行为与静态不同。 当您请求动态页面时，它会不断搜索所有必要的信息和文件。 因此，页面需要更长的时间来加载。 如果您使用缓存插件，则可以减少页面加载所需的时间。 它无疑会提高您的 WordPress 网站的速度。 本质上，缓存插件会复制您的网站和页面。 当您请求页面时，它立即表明您是一个认真的人。 考虑到这一点，您将能够一次处理大量访问者，而无需等待响应。

但是，为了加速 WordPress，您应该使用哪些缓存插件？ 因此，“WP Super Cache”是最好的免费解决方案。 它经过精心设计和优化，您当然可以更改加载时间。


它有助于吸引访问者，80% 的访问者更喜欢视觉内容而不是文本。 同样，图像有可能破坏您的网站。 实际上，没有优化的大图像对任何网站都是不利的。 它可能会减少充电所需的时间。 因此，在提升品牌形象和创建视觉上吸引人的内容时，您应该使用图像，但也应该优化它们。 一般来说，PNG 图像非常适合 Web 发布，但颜色很多，JPEG 格式更可取。 另一方面，切勿使用未经优化的图像。 这将导致 WordPress 变慢。 使用任何图像编辑软件来优化您的网站图像的大小并将其保持在 50KB 以下。 它会让你的网站看起来更轻。 您还可以使用图像优化器插件（例如 ShortPixel Image Optimizer）来自动化该过程。

通常，WordPress 档案和主页、类别和标签会显示每个帖子的完整内容。 它将增加您网站的主页、类别和标签的权重。 这将使您的网站加载时间更长。 同样，您的访问者不需要一直看到您的所有内容。 他们会来选择他们需要的内容。 默认情况下包含这些 WordPress 功能。 你必须个性化它。 转到设置并选择内容摘要以对其进行自定义。 然后它将在整个地方显示内容摘录。 但是，您可以在主题选项中找到它。

您的网站是否收到大量反馈？ 这是您网站受欢迎程度的一个很好的指标。 获得它没有压力。 然而，当谈到加速 WordPress 时，它可能是一个障碍。 因为有很多评论的页面可能需要很长时间才能加载。 另一方面，垃圾邮件可以为您提供。 因此，Akismet 可用于过滤垃圾评论。 同样，要在页面上显示更少的评论，请使用设置中的评论摘录。 不会删除任何评论。 单击下一条或上一条评论将带您到上一条或上一条评论。 具体来说，它将帮助您提高 WordPress 的速度和转化率。

在加载 Facebook 帖子时，Twitter 会跟随按钮、照片、样式表和脚本，许多网站都会进行外部 HTTP 查询。 提高网站的速度并不是一件好事。 如果您的网站一直试图加载大量文件，您的页面速度将会变慢。 现在可以减少外部 HTTP 请求。 视频是一个很好的视觉内容示例，可以让访问者保持兴趣。 它还将帮助您提高转化率。 您将能够将您的视频上传到您的 WordPress 网站，并且会自动创建一个 HTML5 播放器来显示它们。 但是，视频的大小是一个问题。 当您在帖子中包含视频时，您的页面大小会增加。 但是，它将需要更多带宽。 相反，您可以使用 YouTube、Vimeo、DailyMotion 和其他流行的视频共享网站。 这将帮助您缩小页面。 毫无疑问，它将帮助您提高 WordPress 页面的速度。

要启动您的 WordPress 网站，您需要一个主题。 但是，有些主题特别吸引人或与众不同。 但是，它们的编码和优化效率低下。 它可能会减慢加载过程。 实际上，您应该使用编码良好且针对 SEO 优化的主题。 如果你有这样的主题，它将帮助你提高 WordPress 的速度。 更不用说它对 SEO 至关重要。 它也将使您的事情变得更容易。

滑块是通过允许您显示流行或有趣的材料来使您的网站对访问者更具吸引力的最有效方法之一。 但是，写得不好的滑块可能会导致 WordPress 变慢。 本质上，您必须选择一个您能够快速完成的任务。 如果您要使用插件来显示滑块，请确保它已针对速度进行了优化。 但是，您可以根据您的要求咨询其他来源。 使用更快的滑块插件无疑会提高 WordPress 的性能。

长篇文章是权威写作的必备品。 同样，谷歌排名也需要它。 另一方面，长帖子需要更长的时间来加载。 您可以降低页面的速度。 因此，在将帖子分成两页或三页时，您可以使用“下一步”按钮。

它将提高您网站的跳出率。 同样，提高 WordPress 页面的速度将是有益的。

克里斯·邦格斯—— Oct 11 '21 21 年 10 月 11 日



原文:

Priya Rai

Posted on May 27

Do you want to increase or improve WordPress's speed? WordPress, in particular, is currently one of the best and most popular platforms for launching new websites. WordPress is the most widely used blogging platform, with 70% of CMS users using it. The daily amount, on the other hand, continues to rise. However, there is one issue: a large number of users are providing feedback, and WordPress is slow. As a result, they're interested in learning how to make WordPress run faster. You're not aware that WordPress isn't as slow as you believe. It is significantly faster than any other CMS on the market. However, you must ensure that you are speeding up WordPress in the correct manner.

The fundamentals of increasing WordPress's speed

I'll go over some basic questions and answers, as well as ways to speed up WordPress. It will undoubtedly assist you in expanding your knowledge base and learning the fundamentals of increasing WordPress speed. Let's talk about how to properly speed up WordPress.

What are the benefits of having a fast-loading WordPress site?

Do you know that it takes only 7 seconds for a visitor to load a website, and in some cases even less? According to Amazon, if their websites take longer than one second to load, they lose 7% of their conversions. It even results in an 11% drop in page views and a 16% drop in customer satisfaction.
Can you imagine how much money a website owner could lose? Google, on the other hand, considers a slower website to be worthwhile. Similarly, try to stay out of a website's reach. Keeping this in mind, speeding up your WordPress websites is critical.

How to check the speed of your WordPress website?

So, could you tell me how I can test the speed of my WordPress site? Yes! Why should you double-check your websites if you think they load in a matter of seconds? Cookies are small text files that your browser, such as Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox, saves to speed up the loading of a website. New visitors from various locations or computers, on the other hand, will be unable to do so. As a result, you can use Pingdom to test the loading speed of WordPress websites. The first thing to remember is that it's a completely free and extremely useful tool for determining the speed of your WordPress websites.
Remember that Pingdom is a free service that allows you to test your website in different locations. It's worth noting that a website that loads in under two seconds is ideal, and if you can improve even slightly, you'll gain a more loyal customer. Milliseconds, in particular, can have a significant impact on the user experience of your WordPress sites, and you may not even be aware of it.

What slows down the WordPress website?

WordPress websites can be slowed for a variety of reasons. You will get statistics and reasons why your websites are slowing down when you review them. There will also be some technical considerations. However, the reasons listed below are the most common causes of WordPress website slowdown. Website Hosting - If your hosting isn't set up properly, it can cause your websites to load slowly. WordPress Customization – If your websites are not properly customized, they will undoubtedly slow down. You must also install cache plugins and other extensions. Continue reading; I'll explain later. Unoptimized Page – A page with a lot of large images can slow down your website. Unnecessary Plugins – Plugins can cause WordPress to slow down.

External scripts, such as ads and font loaders, can cause WordPress websites to slow down.

Quickly increase the speed of WordPress:

So, you've realized the significance of increasing WordPress's speed. I'll go over how to make WordPress faster in this article. Take the advice and put it into practice on your websites. It will significantly improve WordPress speed and help you rank on Google, as well as change the user experience of your websites.

1. Updating your WordPress site is essential.

Your websites will load very quickly and will be tested in a variety of locations if you use two methods (install cache plugins and optimize images). Similarly, it will not take long to complete. Now it's up to you to keep your WordPress site current. WordPress releases new versions regularly. As a result, it is critical to keep WordPress websites current. It will help you speed up WordPress, improve security, and optimize your site in general. Similarly, updating all plugins regularly will ensure that WordPress runs faster.

2. Install a WordPress caching plugin

It is a simple method that does not require any changes to your website's code and is very effective. Your website page is, in fact, dynamic. As a result, it behaves differently than static. When you request a dynamic page, it constantly searches for all of the necessary information and files. As a result, the page takes longer to load. If you use a caching plugin, you can reduce the time it takes for a page to load. It will undoubtedly improve the speed of your WordPress site. Caching plugins, in essence, make a copy of your website and pages. When you request a page, it immediately demonstrates that you are a serious person. With this in mind, you'll be able to handle a large number of visitors at once without having to wait for a response.

But, to speed up WordPress, which caching plugins should you use? For this reason, " WP Super Cache " is the best free solution. It is extremely well-designed and optimized, and you can certainly alter the loading time.


3. Optimize images for speed Images are the lifeblood of any piece of content, and research shows that visual content can boost conversion rates. It aids in attracting visitors, with 80 percent of visitors preferring visual content to text. Similarly, an image has the potential to destroy your website. In reality, a large image with no optimization is bad for any website. It may reduce the amount of time it takes to charge. So, when it comes to improving your branding and creating visually appealing content, you should use images, but you should also optimize them. In general, a PNG image is excellent for web publishing, but with a lot of colors, a JPEG format is preferable. On the other hand, never use an image that hasn't been optimized. It will cause WordPress to slow down. Use any image editing software to optimize the size of your website image and keep it under 50KB. It will make your website appear lighter. You can also use an image optimizer plugin, such as ShortPixel Image Optimizer, to automate the process.

4. Use excerpts on the home page and in the archives

In general, WordPress archives and home pages, categories, and tags display full content for each post. It will increase the weight of your website's main page, categories, and tags. It will make your websites take longer to load. Likewise, your visitors do not need to see all of your content all of the time. They will come and select the content that they require. These WordPress features are included by default. You must personalize it. Go to Settings and choose a content summary to customize it. It will then display content excerpts all over the place. You will, however, find it in the theme options.

5. Split comments into pages

Are your websites receiving a lot of feedback? It's a good indicator of your website's popularity. There is no pressure to obtain it. However, when it comes to speeding up WordPress, it can be a hindrance. Because a page with a lot of comments can take a long time to load. On the other hand, spam can provide you with it. As a result, Akismet can be used to filter spam comments. Similarly, to display fewer comments on the page, use comment excerpts from the settings. No comments will be removed. Clicking on the next or previous comments will take you to the previous or previous comments. Specifically, it will aid you in increasing WordPress speed and conversion.

6. Reduce External HTTP Requests

When loading Facebook posts, Twitter follows buttons, photos, style sheets, and scripts, many websites make external HTTP queries. Increasing the speed of your websites is not a good thing to do. Your page speed will be slowed if your website attempts to load a large number of files all the time. External HTTP requests can now be reduced. WordPress's speed will undoubtedly improve.

7. Do not upload videos directly to WordPress

Video is a great example of visual content that will keep visitors interested. It will also assist you in increasing your conversion rate. You will be able to upload your videos to your WordPress sites, and an HTML5 player will be created automatically to display them. However, the video's size is an issue. When you include a video in your post, the size of your pages will grow. However, it will require more bandwidth. Instead, you can use YouTube, Vimeo, DailyMotion, and other popular video-sharing sites. This will help you to make your pages smaller. It will undoubtedly assist you in increasing the speed of your WordPress pages.

8. Use a theme that is optimized for WordPress websites that load quickly.

To launch your WordPress websites, you'll need a theme. However, some themes are particularly appealing or distinctive. However, they are inefficiently coded and optimized. It may slow down the loading process. Actually, you should use a theme that is well-coded and optimized for SEO. It will help you increase the speed of WordPress if you get a theme like that. Not to mention the fact that it is critical for SEO. It will also make things easier for you.

9. Use a faster slider plugin

A slider is one of the most effective ways to make your website more appealing to visitors by allowing you to display popular or intriguing material. However, a badly written slider can cause WordPress to slow down. Essentially, you must select one that you are capable of doing rapidly. If you're going to use a plugin to display the slider, make sure it's optimized for speed. Meteor Slides is a free tool that is well-optimized and loads your websites in less than 2 seconds. You can, however, consult another source for your requirements. Using a faster slider plugin would undoubtedly improve WordPress performance.

10. Split Long Posts into Pages

Long posts are a must for authoritative writing. In the same way, it is required for Google ranking. Long posts, on the other hand, take longer to load. You can reduce the pace of the page. As a result, while dividing the post into two or three pages, you can utilize the "Next" button.

It will improve your website's bounce rate. Similarly, increasing the speed of a WordPress page will be beneficial.

Chris Bongers - Oct 11 '21
